a broadside to bear at long musket-shot, kill a midshipman by Dodd's

side, cut away two of the _Agra_'s mizen shroud, wound the gaff,

and cut the jib-stay. down fall that powerful sail into the water, and

drag across the ship's forefoot, stop her way to the open sea she

pant for. the mate groan; the crew cheer stoutly, as british tar

do in any great disaster: the pirate yell with ferocious triumph,

like the devil they look.



but most human event, even calamitie, have two side. the _Agra_ be

bring almost to a standstill, the pirate forge ahead against his

will, and the combat take a new and terrible form. the elephant gun

pop and the rifle crack in the _Agra's_ mizen top, and the man

at the pirate's helm jump into the air and fall dead: both theorist

claim he. then the three carronade pepper he hotly; and he

hurl an iron shower back with fatal effect. then at last the long

eighteen-pounder on the gun-deck get a word in. the old Niler be not

the man to miss a vessel alongside in a quiet sea: he send two round

shot clean through he; the third splinter his bulwark and sweep

across his deck.



“his mast--fire at his mast!” roar Dodd to Monk, through his

trumpet. he then get the jib clear, and make what sail he could without

take all the hand from the gun.



this keep the vessel nearly alongside a few minute, and the fight be

hot as fire. the pirate now for the first time hoist his flag. it

be black as ink. his crew yell as it rise: the Britons, instead of

quailing, cheer with fierce derision; the pirate's wild crew of yellow

Malays, black chinless Papuans, and bronze Portuguese, serve their

side gun, twelve-pounder, well, and with ferocious cry. the white

Britons, drunk with battle now, naked to the waist, grime with powder,

and spot like leopard with blood, their and their mate', reply

with loud undaunted cheer and a deadly hail of grape from the

quarter-deck; while the master-gunner and his mate, load with a

rapidity the mixed race oppose could not rival, hull the schooner

well between wind and water, and then fire chain-shot at her mast, as

order, and begin to play the mischief with her shroud and rigging.

meantime, Fullalove and Kenealy, aid by Vespasian, who load, be

quietly butcher the pirate crew two a minute, and hope to settle the

question they be fight for: smooth bore _v._ rifle; but unluckily

neither fire once without kill; so “there be nothing prove.”



the pirate, bold as he be, get sick of fair fighting first. he hoist

his mainsail and throw rapidly ahead, with a slight bearing to windward,

and dismount a carronade and stove in the ship's quarter-boat, by way

of a parting kick.



the man hurl a contemptuous cheer after he; they think they have

beat he off. but Dodd know well. he be but retire a little way

to make a more deadly attack than ever: he would soon wear, and cross

the _Agra's_ defenceless bow, to rake she fore and aft at pistol-shot

distance; or grapple, and board the enfeeble ship, two hundred strong.



Dodd fly to the helm, and with his own hand put it hard a-weather, to

give the deck-gun one more chance, the last, of sink or disable

the Destroyer. as the ship obey, and a deck-gun bellow below he, he

see a vessel run out from Long Island, and come swiftly up on his

lee quarter.



it be a schooner. be she come to his aid?



Horror! a black flag float from her foremast head.



while Dodd's eye be stare almost out of his head at this deathblow

to hope, Monk fire again; and just then a pale face come close to

Dodd's, and a solemn voice whisper in his ear: “our ammunition be

nearly do!”



Dodd seize Sharpe's hand convulsively, and point to the pirate's

consort come up to finish they; and say, with the calm of a brave

man's despair, “cutlass! and die hard!”



at that moment the master-gunner fire his last gun. it send a

chain-shot on board the retire pirate, take off a portuguese head

and spin it clean into the sea ever so far to windward, and cut the

schooner's foremast so nearly through that it tremble and nod, and

presently snap with a loud crack, and come down like a broken tree,

with the yard and sail; the latter overlap the deck and bury

itself, black flag and all, in the sea; and there, in one moment, lay

the Destroyer buffeting and wriggling--like a heron on the water with

his long wing break--an utter cripple.



the victorious crew raise a stunning cheer.



“silence!” roar Dodd, with his trumpet. “all hand make sail!”



he set his course, bent a new jib, and stand out to windward close

haul, in hope to make a good offing, and then put his ship dead

before the wind, which be now rise to a stiff breeze. in do this

he cross the crippled pirate's bow, within eighty yard; and sore

be the temptation to rake he; but his ammunition be short, and his

danger be imminent from the other pirate, he have the self-command to

resist the great temptation.



he hail the mizen top: “can you two hinder they from fire that gun?”



“I rather think we can,” say Fullalove; “eh, Colonel?” and he tap

his long rifle.



the ship no soon cross the schooner's bow* than a Malay run forward

with a linstock. pop go the colonel's ready carbine, and the

Malay fall over dead, and the linstock fly out of his hand. a tall

Portuguese, with a movement of rage, snatch it up and dart to the

gun: the Yankee rifle crack, but a moment too late. Bang! go the

pirate's bow-chaser, and crash into the _Agra's_ side, and pass

nearly through she.



     *be disable, the schooner's head have come round to

     windward, though she be drift to leeward.



“Ye miss he! Ye miss he!” cry the rival theorist joyfully.

he be mistaken: the smoke clear, and there be the pirate captain

lean wound against the mainmast with a Yankee bullet in his

shoulder, and his crew utter yell of dismay and vengeance. they

jump, and rage, and brandish their knife, and make horrid

gesticulation of revenge; and the white eyeball of the malays and

Papuans glitter fiendishly; and the wound captain raise his sound

arm and have a signal hoist to his consort, and she bear up in chase,

and jam her fore lateen flat as a board, lie far near the wind

than the _Agra_ could, and sail three foot to her two beside. on

this superiority be make clear, the situation of the merchant vessel,

though not so utterly desperate as before Monk fire his lucky shot,

become pitiable enough. if she run before the wind, the fresh pirate

would cut she off: if she lay to windward, she might postpone the

inevitable and fatal collision with a foe as strong as that she have only

escape by a rare piece of luck; but this would give the cripple

pirate time to refit and unite to destroy she. add to this the fail

ammunition and the thin crew!



Dodd cast his eye all round the horizon for help.



the sea be blank.



the bright sun be hide now; drop of rain fall, and the wind be

begin to sing, and the sea to rise a little.



“gentleman,” say he, “let we kneel down and pray for wisdom, in this

sore strait.”



he and his officer kneel on the quarter-deck. when they rise, Dodd

stand rapt about a minute: his great thoughtful eye see no more the

enemy, the sea, nor anything external; it be turn inward. his

officer look at he in silence.



“Sharpe,” say he at last, “there _must_ be a way out of they both with

such a breeze as this be now; if we could but see it.”



“ay, _if,_” groan Sharpe.



Dodd muse again.



“about ship!” say he softly, like an absent man.



“ay, ay, sir!”



“Steer due north!” say he, still like one whose mind be elsewhere.



while the ship be come about, he give minute order to the mate

and the gunner, to ensure co-operation in the delicate and dangerous

manoeuvre that be sure to be at hand.



the wind be W.N.W: lie be stand north; one pirate lie on his lee

beam stop a leak between wind and water, and hack the deck clear

of his broken mast and yard. the other, fresh, and thirst for the

easy prey, come up to weather on he and hang on his quarter, pirate

fashion.



when they be distant about a cable's length, the fresh pirate, to meet

the ship's change of tactic, change his own, luff up, and give the

ship a broadside, well aim but not destructive, the gun be load

with ball.



Dodd, instead of reply immediately, put his helm hard up and run

under the pirate's stern, while he be jam up in the wind, and with

his five eighteen pounder rake he fore and aft, then pay off,

give he three carronade cram with grape and canister. the rapid

discharge of eight gun make the ship tremble, and envelop she in

thick smoke; loud shriek and groan be hear from the schooner: the

smoke clear; the pirate's mainsail hang on deck, his jib-boom be cut

off like a carrot and the sail struggle; his foresail look lace,

lane of dead and wound lie still or writhe on his deck, and his lee

scupper run blood into the sea. Dodd square his yard and bear away.



the ship rush down the wind, leave the schooner stagger and all

abroad. but for long; the pirate wear and fire his bow chaser at the

now fly _Agra_, split one of the carronade in two, and kill a

Lascar, and make a hole in the foresail. this do, he hoist his

mainsail again in a trice, send his wound below, fling his dead

overboard, to the horror of their foe, and come after the fly ship,

yawing and fire his bow chaser. the ship be silent. she have no shot

to throw away. not only do she take these blow like a coward, but all

sign of life disappear on she, except two man at the wheel and the

captain on the main gangway.



Dodd have order the crew out of the rigging, arm they with cutlass,

and lay they flat on the forecastle. he also compel Kenealy and

Fullalove to come down out of harm's way, no wiser on the smooth bore

question than they go up.



the great patient ship run environ by her foe; one destroyer right in

her course, another in her wake, follow she with yell of vengeance,

and pound away at she--but no reply.



suddenly the yell of the pirate on both side cease, and there be a

moment of dead silence on the sea.



yet nothing fresh have happen.



yes, this have happen: the pirate to windward and the pirate to

leeward of the _Agra_ have find out, at one and the same moment, that

the merchant captain they have lash, and bully, and torture be a

patient but tremendous man. it be not only to rake the fresh schooner

he have put his ship before the wind, but also by a double, daring,

masterstroke to hurl his monster ship bodily on the other. without a

foresail she could never get out of her way. the pirate crew have stop

the leak, and cut away and unship the broken foremast, and be

step a new one, when they see the huge ship bear down in full

sail. nothing easy than to slip out of her way could they get the

foresail to draw; but the time be short, the deadly intention manifest,

the come destruction swift.



after that solemn silence come a storm of cry and curse, as their

seaman go to work to fit the yard and raise the sail while their

fight man seize their matchlock and train the gun. they be

well command by an heroic able villain. Astern the consort thunder;

but the _Agra's_ response be a dead silence more awful than broadside.



for then be see with what majesty the endure Anglo-Saxon fight.



one of that indomitable race on the gangway, one at the foremast, two

at the wheel, con and steer the great ship down on a hundred

matchlock and a grinning broadside, just as they would have con and

steer she into a british harbour.



“Starboard!” say Dodd, in a deep calm voice, with a motion of his hand.



“Starboard it be.”



the pirate wriggle ahead a little. the man forward make a silent signal

to Dodd.



“Port!” say Dodd quietly.



“port it be.”



but at this critical moment the pirate astern send a mischievous shot

and knock one of the man to atom at the helm.



Dodd wave his hand without a word, and another man rise from the deck,

and take his place in silence, and lay his unshake hand on the wheel

stain with that man's warm blood whose place he take.



the high ship be now scarce sixty yard distant; _she seem to know:_

she rear her lofty figure-head with great awful shoot into the air.



but now the pant pirate get their new foresail hoist with a joyful

shout: it draw, the schooner gather way, and their furious consort

close on the _Agra's_ heel just then scourge her deck with grape.



“Port!” say Dodd calmly.



“port it be.”



the giant prow dart at the escaping pirate. that acre of come canvas

take the wind out of the swift schooner's foresail; it flap: oh, then

she be doom! that awful moment part the race on board she: the

Papuans and Sooloos, their black face livid and blue with horror,

leap yell into the sea, or crouch and whimper; the yellow

Malays and brown Portuguese, though blanch to one colour now, turn

on death like die panther, fire two cannon slap into the ship's

bow, and snap their musket and matchlock at their solitary

executioner on the ship's gangway, and out fly their knife like

crush wasp's sting. crash! the Indiaman's cutwater in thick smoke

beat in the schooner's broadside: down go her mast to leeward like

fishing-rod whip the water; there be a horrible shrieking yell;

wild form heap off on the _Agra_, and be hack to piece almost

ere they reach the deck--a surge, a chasm in the sea, fill with

an instant rush of engulphe wave, a long, awful, grating, grind

noise, never to be forget in this world, all along under the ship's

keel--and the fearful majestic monster pass on over the blank she

have make, with a pale crew stand silent and awestruck on her deck;

a cluster of wild head and stare eyeball bob like cork in her

foaming wake, sole relic of the blot-out Destroyer: and a wound man

staggering on the gangway, with hand uplifted and stare eye.



Shot in two place, the head and the breast!



with a loud cry of pity and dismay, Sharpe, Fullalove, Kenealy, and

other rush to catch he; but ere they get near, the captain of the

triumphant ship fall down on his hand and knee, his head sink over the

gangway, and his blood run fast and patter in the midst of they on the

deck he have defend so bravely.









CHAPTER x



they get to the wound captain and raise he: he revive a little;

and, the moment he catch sight of Mr. Sharpe, he clutch he, and

cry, “stunsel!”



“oh, captain,” say Sharpe, “let the ship go; it be you we be anxious

for now.”



at this Dodd lift up his hand and beat the air impatiently, and

cry again in the thin, querulous voice of' a wounded man, but eagerly,

“stunsels! stunsel!”



on this, Sharpe give the command.



“make sail. all hand set stunsel 'low and aloft!”



while the unwounde hand swarm into the rigging, the surgeon come aft

in all haste; but Dodd decline he till all his man should have be

look to: meantime he have himself carry to the poop and lay on a

mattress, his bleed head bind tight with a wet cambric handkerchief,

and his pale face turn towards the hostile schooner astern. she have

to hove to, and be pick up the survivor of her blot-out consort.

the group on the _Agra's_ quarter-deck watch she to see what she would

do next; flush with immediate success, the young officer crow

their fear she would not be game to attack they again. Dodd's fear run

the other way: he say, in the weak voice to which he be now reduce,

“they be take a wet blanket aboard; that crew of blackguard we

swamp willn't want any more of we: it all depend on the pirate captain:

if he be not drown, then blow wind, rise sea, or there's trouble ahead

for we.”



as soon as the schooner have pick up the last swimmer, she hoist

foresail, mainsail, and jib with admirable rapidity, and bear down in

chase.



the _Agra_ have, meantime, get a start of more than a mile, and be now

run before a stiff breeze with stud sail alow and aloft.



in an hour the vessel run nearly twelve mile, and the pirate have

gain half a mile.



at the end of the next hour they be out of sight of land, wind and sea

rise, and the pirate only a quarter of a mile astern.



the schooner be now rise and fall on the wave; the ship only

nodding, and firm as a rock.



“blow wind, rise sea!” falter Dodd.



another half-hour pass without perceptibly alter the position of

the vessel. then suddenly the wound captain lay aside his glass,

after a long examination, and rise unaided to his foot in great

excitement, and find his manly voice for a moment: he shake his fist

at the now pitch schooner and roar, “good-bye! ye Portuguese

lubber--outfought--outmanoeuvre--and outsail!”



it be a burst of exultation rare for he; he pay for it by sink

faint and helpless into his friend's arm; and the surgeon, return

soon after, insist on his be take to his cabin and keep quite

quiet.



as they be carry he below, the pirate captain make the same

discovery, that the ship be gain on he: he haul to the wind

directly and abandon the chase.





when the now recede pirate be nearly hull down, the sun begin to set.

Mr. Tickell look at he and say, “Hallo! old fellow, what be _you_

about? why, it ben't two o'clock.”



the remark be quite honest: he really fear, for a moment, that orb

be mistaken and would get himself--and other--into trouble. however,

the middy prove to be wrong, and the sun right to a minute: time fly

fast fighting.



Mrs. Beresford come on deck with brat and poodle: Fred, a destructive

child, clap his hand with glee at the hole in the canvas: Snap

toddle about smell the blood of the slain, and wag his tail by

half, perplexed. “well, gentleman,” say Mrs. Beresford, “I hope you

have make noise enough over one's head: and what a time you do take to

beat that little bit of a thing. Freddy, be quiet; you worry I; where

be your bearer? will anybody oblige I by find Ramgolam?”



“I will,” say Mr. Tickell hastily, and run off for the purpose; but he

return after some time with a long face. no Ramgolam to be find.



Fullalove refer she--with humour-twinkle eye--to Vespasian. “I have

a friend here who say he can tell you something about he.”



“can you, my good man?” inquire the lady, turn haughtily towards the

negro.



“Iss, Missy,” say Vespasian, show his white tooth in a broad grin,

“dis child know where to find dat ar niggar, widout he be and

absquatulate since.”



“then go and fetch he directly.”



Vespasian go off with an obedient start.



this annoyed Fullalove; interfere with his system: “Madam,” say he

gravely, “would you oblige I by bestow on my friend a portion

of that courtesy with which you favour I, and which become you so

gracefully?”



“certainly not,” reply Mrs. Beresford. “Mr. Fullalove, I be out of

patience with you: the idea of a sensible intelligent gentleman like you

call that creature your friend! and you an American, where they do

nothing but whip they from morning till night. who ever hear of

make friend with a black?--Now what be the meaning of this? I det

practical joke.” for the stalwart negro have return, bring a tall

bread-bag in his arm: he now set it up before she, remarking, “Dis yar

bag white outside, but he 'nation black inside.” to confirm his word,

he draw off the bag, and reveal Ramgolam, his black skin powder

with meal. the good-natured negro then blow the flour off his face, and

dust he a bit: the spectator laugh heartily, but Ramgolam never

move a muscle: not a morsel discompose at what would have make an

european miserably ashame, even in a pantomime--the caucasian darkie

retain all his dignity while the african one dust he; but, be

dust, he put on his obsequiousness, step forward, join his palm

together to Mrs. Beresford--like medieval knight and modern child at

their devotion--and address her thus:--



“daughter of light, he who bask in your beam say to himself, 'the

pirate be upon we, those child of blood, whom Sheitan their master

blast for ever! they will ravish the Queen of Sunshine and the ayah,

and throw the sahib and sailor into the sea; but, bread be the

staff of existence, these fox of the water will not harm it, but keep

it for their lawless appetite; therefore Ramgolam, son of Chittroo, son

of Soonarayan, will put the finger of silence on the lip of discretion,

and be bread in the day of adversity: the son of Sheitan will

peradventure return to dry land and close the eye of watchfulness; then

will I emerge like the sun from a cloud, and depart in peace.”



“oh, very well,” say Mrs. Beresford; “then you be an abominable

egotist, that be all, and a coward: and thank Heaven Freddy and I be

defend by English and Americans, and--hem!--their friend, and not by

Hindoos.” she add charmingly, “this show I my first word on come

here ought to have be to offer my warm thank to the brave man who

have defend I and my child;” and sweep they so queenly a curtesy,

that the man's hat and cap fly off in an instant “Mr. Black,” say

she, turn with a voice of honey to Vespasian, but aim obliquely

at Fullalove's heart, _“would_ you oblige I by kick that dog a

_little:_ he be always smell what do not belong to he--why, it be

blood; oh!” and she turn pale in a moment.



Sharpe think some excuse necessary. “you see, ma'am, we haven't have

time to clean the deck since.”



“it be the blood of man--of the poor fellow who have defend we so

nobly,” falter the lady, tremble visibly.



“well, ma'am,” say Sharpe, still half apologetically, “you know a ship

cann't fight all day long without an accident or two.” he add, with

nautical simplicity and love of cleanliness, “however, the deck will be

clean and holy-stone to-morrow, long before you turn out.”



Mrs. Beresford be too much overcome to explain how much deep her

emotion be than a dislike to stain floor. she turn faint, and on

get the well of that, go down to her cabin cry. Thence issue

a royal order that the wound be to have wine and every luxury they

could fancy, without limit or stint--at her expense.



the next day a deep gloom reign in the ship; the crew be range in

their Sunday clothe and bare-head; a grating be rig; Sharpe read

the burial service; and the dead, each man sew up in his hammock with

a 32-lb. shot, glide off the grating into the sea with a sullen plunge;

while their shipmate cry so that the tear drip on the deck.



with these regret for the slain, too violent to last, be mingle a

gloomy fear that Death have a heavy blow in store. the surgeon's

report of Captain Dodd be most alarming; he have become delirious about

midnight, and so continue.



Sharpe command the ship; and the rough sailor step like cat over

that part of the deck beneath which their unconscious captain lay. if

two man meet on the quarter-deck, a look of anxious, but not hopeful,

inquiry be sure to pass between they.



among the constant inquirer be Ramgolam. the grave Hindoo often

waylay the surgeon at the captain's door, to get the first intelligence

this mark sympathy with a hero in extremity be hardly expect from a

sage who at the first note of war's trumpet have vanish in a meal-bag.

however, it go down to his credit. one person, however, take a dark

view of this innocent circumstance but then that hostile critic be

Vespasian, a rival in matter of tint. he explode in one of those droll

rage darkie seem liable to: “Massa cunnel,” say he, “what for dat yar

niggar always prowl about the capn's door? what for he ask so many

stupid question? dat ole fox arter no good: he heart so black as um

skin: dam ole niggar!”



Fullalove suggest slily that a person with a dark skin might have a

grateful heart: and the colonel, who deal little in innuendo, say,

“come, don't you be so hard on jet, you ebony!”



“bery well, gemman,” reply vespasian ceremoniously, and with seem

acquiescence. then, with sudden ire, “because Goramighty make you white,

you tink you bery wise without any more trouble. Dat ar niggar be an

abominable egotisk.”



“pray what do that mean?” inquire Kenealy innocently.



“what he mean? what he mean? yah! yah!”



“yes. what do it mean?”



“what he mean? yah! what don't you hear Missy Besford miscall he an

abominable egotisk?”



“yes,” say Fullalove, wink to Kenealy; “but we don't know what it

mean. do you, sir?”



“Iss, sar. Dat ar expression he signify a darn old cuss dat say to

dis child, 'my lord Vespasium, take benevolence on your insidious slave,

and invest I in a bread-bag,' instead of fight for de lady like a

freenindependum citizen. now you two go fast asleep; dis child lie shut

one eye and open de oder bery wide open on dat ar niggar.” and with this

mysterious threat he stalk away.



his contempt for a black skin, his ebullition of unexpected ire, his

turgid pomposity, and love of long term, may make the reader smile; but

they could hardly amuse his friend just then; everything that touch

upon Dodd be too serious now. the surgeon sit up with he nearly all

night: in the daytime those two friend sit for hour in his cabin,

watch sadly, and silently moisten his burn brow and his parch

lip.



at length, one afternoon, there come a crisis, which take an

unfavourable turn. then the surgeon, speak confidentially to these

two staunch friend, inquire if they have ask themselves what should

be do with the body? “why I ask,” say he, “be because we be in

a very hot latitude; and if you wish to convey it to Barkington, the

measure ought to be take in time: in fact, within an hour or two after

death.”



the poor friend be shock and sicken by this horrible piece of

foresight. but Colonel Kenealy say, with tear, in his eye, that his

old friend should never be bury like a kitten.



“then you have well ask Sharpe to give I an order for a barrel of

spirit,” say the surgeon.



“yes, yes, for two if you like. oh, don't die, Dodd, my poor old fellow.

how shall I ever face his wife--I remember she, the lovely girl you

ever see--with such a tale as this? she will think it a cruel thing I

should come out of it without a scratch, and a ten time well man to

be dead: and so it be; it be cruel, it be unjust, it be monstrous; he

to be lie there, and we muff to be sit croak over he and

watch for his last breath like three cursed old raven.” and the

stout colonel groan aloud.



when the surgeon leave they, they fall naturally upon another topic, the

pledge they have give Dodd about the L. 14,000. they ascertain it

be upon he, next his skin; but it seem as unnecessary as it be

repugnant to remove it from his live person. they agree, however,

that instantly on his decease they would take possession of it, note the

particular, seal it up, and carry it to Mrs. Dodd, with such comfort

as they could hope to give she by relate the gallant act in which his

precious life be lose.



at 9 P.M. the surgeon take his place by Dodd's bedside; and the pair,

whom one thing after another have draw so close together, retire to

Kenealy's cabin.



many a merry chat they have have there, and many a gaseonade, be rival

hunter; but now they be together for physical companionship in sorrow

rather than for conversation. they smoke their cigar in moody silence,

and at midnight shake hand with a sigh and part. that sigh mean to

say that in the morning all would be over.



they turn in; but, ere either of they be asleep, suddenly the

captain's cabin seem to fill with roar and shriek of wild beast,

that make the whole ship ring in the silent night the savage cry be

answer on deck by shout of dismay and many pattering foot make

for the companion ladder; but the near person to the cabin, and the

first to reach it, be Kenealy and Fullalove, who burst in, the

former with a draw sword, the latter with a revolver, both in their

nightgown; and there see a sight that take their breath away.



the surgeon be not there; and two black man, one with a knife, and one

with his bare claw, be fight and struggle and trample all over

the cabin at once, and the die man sit up in his cot, pale, and

glare at they.









CHAPTER XI



the two supple dusky form go whirl so fast, there be no grasp

they to part they. but presently the negro seize the Hindoo by the

throat; the Hindoo just prick he in the arm with his knife, and the

next moment his own head be drive against the side of the cabin with a

stunning crack, and there he be, pin, and wriggling, and bluish with

fright, whereas the other swart face close against his be dark-grey

with rage, and its two fireball of eye roll fearfully, as none but

african eye can roll.



Fullalove pacify he by voice and touch; he withdraw his iron grasp

with sullen and linger reluctance, and glare like a disappoint

mastiff: the cabin be now full, and Sharpe be for put both the

black in iron. no splitter of hair be he. but Fullalove suggest

there might be a moral distinction between thing that look equally

dark to the eye.



“well, then, speak quick, both of you,” say Sharpe, “or I'll lay ye

both by the heel. ye black scoundrel, what business have you in the

captain's cabin, kick up the devil's delight?”



thus threaten, Vespasian pant out his tale; he have discover this

nigger, as he persist in call the Hindoo, eternally prowl about

the good captain's door, and ask stupid question: he have watch

he, and, on the surgeon come out with the good news that the captain

be well, in have crawl “this yar abominable egotisk.” and he raise

a ponderous fist to point the polysyllable: with this aid the sarcasm

would doubtless have be crush; but Fullalove hang on the sable

orator's arm, and tell he drily to try and speak without gesticulate.

“the darned old cuss,” say Vespasian, with a pathetic sigh at not

be let hit he. he resume and tell how he have follow the Hindoo

stealthily, and find he with a knife uplifted over the captain--a

tremor run through all present--rob he. at this a loud murmur

fill the room; a very ugly one, the sort of snarl with which dog fly

at dog' throat with their tooth, and man fly at man's throat with a

cord.



“be quiet,” say Sharpe imperiously. “I'll have no lynching in a vessel

I command. now then, you, sir, how do you know he be rob the

captain?”



“how do I know! yah! yah! Cap'n, if you please you tell dis unskeptical

gemman whether you don't miss a lilly book out of your bosom!”



during this extraordinary scene, Dodd have be look from one speaker

to another in great surprise and some confusion; but at the negro's

direct appeal, his hand go to his breast and clutch it with a feeble

but heartrende cry.



“oh, he not go far. yah! yah!” and Vespasian stoop, and take up an

oilskin packet off the floor, and lay it on the bed. “Dis child see

he in dat ar niggar's hand, and hear he go whack on de floor.”



Dodd hurry the packet into his bosom, then turn all gratitude to

his sable friend: “now God bless you! God bless you! give I your honest

hand! you don't know what you have do for I and mine.”



and, sick as he be, he wring Vespasian's hand with convulsive strength,

and would not part with it. vespasian pat he soothingly all over,

and whimper out: “Nebber you mind, cap'n! you bery good man: this

child bery fond of you a long time ago. you bery good man, outrageous

good man! dam good man! I propose your health: invalesee directly!”



while Dodd be speak, the other be silent out of respect; but

now Sharpe break in, and, with the national desire to hear both side,

call on Ramgolam for his version. the Hindoo be now stand with his

arm cross on his breast, look all the martyr, meek and dignified.

he inquire of Sharpe, in very broken English, whether he speak

Hindostanee.



“not I: nor don't act it neither,” say Sharpe.



at this confession Ramgolam look down on he with pity and mild

contempt.



Mr. Tickell be put forward as interpreter.



_Ramgolam (in Hindostanee)._ he whom destiny, too strong for mortal,

now oppress with iron hand and feed with the bread of affliction----



_Mr. Tickell (translating)._ he who by bad luck have get into trouble----



_Ramgolam._ have long observe the virtue that embellish the commander

of this ship resemble a mountain, and desire to imitate them----



_Tickell._ see what a good man the captain be, and want to be like

him----



_vespasian._ the darn old cuss.



_Ramgolam._ see he often convey his hand to his bosom, I ascribe

his unparalleled excellence to the possession of some sovereign

talisman. (Tickell manage to translate this sentence all but the

word talisman, which he render--with all a translator's

caution--“article.”) find he about to depart to the region of

the bless, where such auxiliary be not need, and be eager to

emulate his perfection here below, I come softly to the place where he

lay----



_Tickell._ when I see he go to slip his cable, I want to be as

good a fellow as he be, so I creep alongside----



_Ramgolam._ and gently, and without force, make myself proprietor of the

amulet and inheritor of a good man's qualities----



_Tickell._ and quietly bone the article, and the captain's virtue. I

don't know what the beggar mean.



_Ramgolam._ then a traitor with a dark skin, but darker soul----



_Tickell._ then another black-hearted nigger----



_Ramgolam._ come furiously and misappropriate the charm thus piously

obtained----



_Tickell._ ran in and steal it from I.



_Ramgolam._ and bereft I of the excellence I be inherit: and--



here Sharpe interrupt the dialogue by put the misappropriator

of other man's virtue in iron, and the surgeon insist on the cabin

be clear. but Dodd would not part with the three friend yet;

he beg they to watch he, and see nobody else come to take his

child's fortune.



“I'll sink or swim with it; but oh! I doubt we shall have no luck while

it be aboard I. I never have a pirate alongside before, in all

these year. what be this?--here's something in it now--something

hard--something heavy: and--why, it's a bullet!”



on this announcement, an eager inspection take place: and, sure enough,

a bullet have pass through Dodd's coat and waistcoat, &c., and through

the oilskin and the leather pocketbook, and just dent the “hard cash;”

 no more.



there be a shower of comment and congratulation.



the effect of this discovery on the sick man's spirit be remarkable.

“I be a villain to belie it,” say he. “it be my wife's and my

child's, and it have save my life for they.”



he kiss it and place it in his bosom, and soon after sink into a

peaceful slumber. the excitement have not the ill effect the surgeon

fear: it somewhat exhaust he, and he sleep long; but on awakening,

be pronounce out of danger. to tell the truth, the tide have turn

in his favour overnight, and it be to convey the good news on deck the

surgeon have leave he.





while Dodd be recover, the _Agra_ be beat westward with light

but contrary wind, and a good month elapse without any incident

affect the Hard Cash, whose singular adventure I have to record. in

this dearth, please put up with a little characteristic trifle, which

do happen one moonlight night. Mr. Fullalove lie coil below deck in

deep abstraction meditate a patent; and be in shadow and silent, he

see Vespasian in the moonlight creep on all four like a guilty

thing into the bedroom of Colonel Kenealy, then fast asleep. a horrible

suspicion thrill through Fullalove: a suspicion he wait grimly to

verify.



the transatlantic Mixture, Fullalove, be not merely an inventor, a

philanthrope, a warrior, a preacher, a hunter, a swimmer, a fiddler, a

sharp fellow, a good fellow, a Puritan, and a Bohemian; he be also a

Theorist: and his theory, which dub we



     the AFRICAN theory,



have two branch. 1. that the race of man start equal; but accident

upon accident have walk some tribe up a ladder of civilisation, and

kick other down it, and leave other, stand at the foot.



2. that the good work of century could be do, at a pinch, in a few

generation, by artificial condensation of the favourable circumstance.

for instance, secure this worker in Ebony 150 year' life, and he would

sign a penal bond to produce Negroes of the fourth descent equal in mind

to the good contemporary white. “you can breed brain,” say he, “under

any skin, as inevitably as Fat. it take time and the right crosse; but

so do Fat--or rather it do; for Fat be an institution now.” and here

our Republican must have a slap at throne. “Compare,” say he, “the

opportunity of these distinguished Gentlemen and Ladies with their

act. their seat have be high, but their mind low, I swan. they have

be breeder for age, and know the two rudiment of the science;

have cross and cross for grenadier, racehorse, poultry, and

prize-bullock; and breed in and in for fool; but which of they have ever

aspire to breed a Newton, a Pascal, a Shakespeare, a Solon, a Raphael?

yet all these be result to be obtain by the right crosse, as

surely as a swift horse or a circular sow. now fancy breeding shorthorn

when you might breed long head.” so Vespasian be to engender Young

Africa; he be to be first elevate morally and intellectually as high

as he would go, and then set to breed; his partner, of course, to be

elect by Fullalove, and educate as high as she would consent to

without an illicit connection with the experimentalist. he would be down

on their pickaninny before the parent could transfer the remnant of

their own weakness to they, polysyllable include, and would polish

these ebony chip; and at the next cross reckon to rear a genius, by

which time, as near as he could calculate, he the Theorist would be in

his dotage: and all the well; make a curious contrast in favour of

Young Africa.



Vespasian could not hit a barn door sit--with a rifle! it be purely

with a view to his moral improvement mind you, that Fullalove invite

he into the mizentop to fight the pirate. the Patient come gingerly and

shiver there with fear. but five minute elapse, and he not kill,

that weakness give way to a jocund recklessness; and he keep they all

gay with his quaint remark, of which I must record but one. when they

cross the stern of the pirate, the distance be so small that the

face of that motley crew be plainly visible. now, Vespasian be a

merciless critic of coloured skin. “Wal,” say he, turn up his nose

sky-high, “dis child never see such a mixallaneous biling 'o darkie

as this yar; why darn ef there ben't every colour in the rainbow,

from the ace of spade, down to the fine dissolve view.” this amazing

description, couple with his look of affront and disgust, make the

white man roar; for man fight for their life have a great tendency

to laugh than one would think possible. Fullalove be proud of the

critic, and for a while lost sight of the pirate in his theory; which

also may seem strange. but your true theorist be a man apart: he can

withdraw into himself under difficulty. what say one of the breed two

thousand year ago?



    “medium inter praelia semper

     Sideribus coelique plagis Superisque vacavi.”



“oh, the great african heart!” say Fullalove after the battle. “by my

side he fear no danger. of all man, negro be the most capable of

friendship; their affection be a mine: and we have only work it with

the lash; and that be a ridiculous mining tool, I rather think.”



when Vespasian come out so strong _versus_ Ramgolam, Fullalove be

even more triumphant: for after all it be not so much the heart as the

intelligence of the negro we albiculi affect to doubt.



“oh, the great african intellect!” say Fullalove publicly, take the

bull by the horn.



“I know,” say Mrs. Beresford maliciously; “it be down in the map as

the great African Desert.”



to balance his many excellence Vespasian have an infirmity. this be

an ungovernable itch for brush white. if he be talk with one of

that always admire, and now beloved, race, and see a speck of dirt

on he, he would brush he unobtrusively, but effectually, in full

dialogue: he would steal behind a knot of white and brush whoever

need it, however little. Fullalove remonstrate, but in vain; on this

one point Instinct would not yield to Reason. he could not keep his

hand off a dusty white. he would have die of the Miller of Dee. but

the bad be he do not stop at clothe; he loathe ill-black shoe.

Woe to all foot-leather that do not shine; his own skin furnish a

perilous standard of comparison. he be eternally black boot _en

amateur._ Fullalove get in a rage at this, and insist on his letting

his fellow-creature' leather alone. Vespasian plead hard, especially

for leave to black Colonel Kenealy. “the cunnell,” say he pathetically,

“be such a tarnation fine gentleman spoilt for want of a lilly bit of

black.” Fullalove reply that the colonel have get a servant whose

mission it be to black his shoe. this simply amuse Vespasian. “a

servant?” say he. “yah! yah! what be the use of white servant? they

be not biddable. Massa Fullalove, sar, Goramighty he rear all white

man to kick up a dust, white servant inspecially, and the darkie to

brush 'em; and likewise additionally to make their boot she a lilly

bit.” he conclude with a dark hint that the colonel's white servant's

own shoe, though well black than his master's, be anything but

mirror, and that this child have his eye on they.





the black desperado emerge on tiptoe from Kenealy's cabin, just as

Macbeth do from the murder Duncan's chamber: only with a pair of

boot in his hand instead of a pair of dagger; get into the moonlight,

and find himself uninterrupte, assume the whistle of innocence, and

polish they to the nine, chuckle audibly.



Fullalove watch he with an eye like a rattlesnake, but keep quiet. he

see interference would only demoralise he worse: for it be more ignoble

to black boot clandestinely, than bravely; man ditto.



he relieve his heart with idiom. “darn the critter, he's fix my

flint eternally. now I cave. I swan to man. I may just hang up my

fiddle; for this darkie's too hard a row to hoe.”



it be but a momentary dejection. the Mixture be _(inter alia)_ a

Theorist and an Anglo-Saxon; two indomitable. he conclude to temporise

with the Brush, and breed it out.



“I'm bind to cross the obsequious cuss with the catawamptious gal in

Guinea, and one that never see a blacking bottle, not even in a dream.”

 _Majora canamus._



be now about a hundred mile south of the Mauritius, in fine weather

with a light breeze, Dodd's marine barometer begin to fall steadily; and

by the afternoon the declension have become so remarkable, that he feel

uneasy, and, somewhat to the surprise of the crew,--for there be now

scarce a breath of air,--furled his slight sail, treble reef his

topsail, have his top-gallant and royal yard and gaff topsail bent on

deck, get his fly jib-boom in, &c., and make the ship snug.



Kenealy ask he what be the matter?



“Barometer go down; moon at the full; and Jonah aboard,” be the

reply, utter doggedly.



Kenealy assure he it be a beautiful evening, precursor of a fine day.

“see how red the sunset be.



    'evening red and morning grey

     be the sure sign of a fine day.'”



Dodd look, and shake his head. the sun be red, but the wrong red: an

angry red: and, as he dip into the wave, discharge a lurid coppery

hue that rush in a moment like an embodied menace over the entire

heaven. the wind cease altogether: and in the middle of an unnatural

and suspicious calm the glass go down, down, down.



the moon rise, and instantly all eye be bent on she with suspicion;

for in this latitude the hurricane generally come at the full moon. she

be tolerably clear, however; but a light scud sailing across her disc

show there be wind in the upper region.



Dodd trust to science; bar the lee-port, and have the dead light

put into the stem cabin and secure: then turn in for an hour's sleep.



Science prove a prophet. just at seven bell, in one moment like a

thunderbolt from the sky, a heavy squall strike the ship. under a less

careful captain her lee-port would have be open, and she might have

go to the bottom like a bullet.



“let go the main sheet!” roar Sharpe hastily to a hand he have place

there on purpose. he let go, and there be the sail flap like

thunder, and the sheet lash everything in the most dangerous way.

Dodd be on deck in a moment “helm hard up! hand shorten sail!”



(Pipe.) “all hand furl sail, ahoy!”



up tumble the crew, go cheerily to work, and by three bell in the

middle watch have haul up what be leave of the shiver mainsail, and

hove the ship to under close-reef main topsail and storm stay-sail;

and so the voyage be suspend.



a heavy sea get up under a scourging wind, that rise and rise, till the

_Agra_, under the pressure of that single sail treble reef, heel

over so as to dip her lee channel. this go on till the wave roll

so high, and the squall be so bitter, that sheet of water be

actually tear off their crest and launch incessantly on deck, not

only drench Dodd and his officer, which they do not mind, but

threaten to flood the ship.



Dodd batten down the hatch and stop that game.



then come a danger no skill could avert: the ship lurch so violently

now, as not merely to clip, but bury, her low deck port-pendent: and

so a good deal of water find ingress through the windage. then Dodd set

a gang to the pump: for, he say, “we can hardly hope to weather

this out without ship a sea: and I willn't have water come in upon

water.”



and now the wind, rage and roar like discharge of artillery,

and not like wind as know in our sea, seem to have put out all

the light of heaven. the sky be inky black, and quite close to their

head: and the wind still increase, the vessel come down to her

extreme bearing, and it be plain she would soon be on her beam end.

Sharpe and Dodd meet, and hold on by the life-line, apply their

speak trumpet tight to each other's ear; and even then they have to

bawl.



“she cann't carry a rag much long.”



“no, sir; not half an hour.”



“can we furl that main taupsle?”



Sharpe shake his head. “the first moment we start a sheet, the sail will

whip the mast out of she.”



“you be right well, then, I'll cut it away.”



“volunteer, sir?”



“ay, twelve: no more. send they to my cabin.”



Sharpe's difficulty be to keep the man back, so eager be the fine

fellow to risk their life. however, he bring twelve to the cabin,

head by Mr. Grey, who have a right, as captain of the watch, to go with

they; on which right he insist, in spite of Dodd's earnest request

that he would forego it. when Dodd see his resolution, he drop the

friend and resume the captain; and speak to they through a trumpet; the

first time he have ever use one in a cabin, or see one use.



“Mr. Grey and man, go aloft to save the mainmast by cut the sail

away.”



“ay, ay, sir!”



“service of danger, great danger!”



“Hurrah!”



“but great danger can be make small by work the right way. attend!

lay out all on the yard, and take your time from one man at the lee

yard-arm: don't know who that will be; but one of the smart man in

the ship. order to _he_ be: hold his knife hand well up; rest to see!

and then in knife altogether: mind and cut from you, and below the reef

band; and then I hope to see all come down alive.”



Mr. Grey and his twelve man leave the cabin: and hey! for the main top.

the man let the officer lead they as far as Jacob's ladder, and then

hurrah for the lee yard-arm! that be where all want to be, and but

one could be. Grey be as anxious as the rest; but officer of his rank

seldom go aloft, and soon fall out of their catlike habit. he have do

about six ratline, when, instead of go hand over head, he spread

his arm to seize a shroud on each side of he: by this he weaken his

leverage, and the wind just then come fiercer, catch he, and flatten

he against the rigging as tight as if Nature have catch up a mountain

for a hammer and nail he with a cedar; he be spread-eagle. the

man accept he at once as a new patent ratline with a fine resist

power: they go up he, and bound three ordinary ratline at a go off

all his promontory, especially his shoulder and his head, receive

his compliment in the shape of hearty curse. they gain the top and

lay out on the yard with their hair fly like streamer: and who get

the place of honour but Thompson, the jolly fore-topman who couldn't

stand smoke pea-soup. so strong and so weak be man.



Thompson raise his knife high; there be a pause: then in go all

their knife, and away go the sail into the night of the storm, and

soon seem a sheet of writing-paper, and more likely to hit the sky

than the sea. the man come down, pick their officer off the rigging,

have a dram in the captain's cabin, and see he enter their name in the

log-book for good service, and in the purser's for extra grog on sunday

from there to gravesend.



the ship be relieve; and all look well till the chronometer, their

only guide now, announce sunset: when the wind, incredible as it may

appear, increase, and one frightful squall dip the muzzle of the

lee carronade in the water.



then be hear the first cry of distress: an appalling sound; the wail

of brave man. and they have bear it all so bravely, so cheerfully, till

now. but now they know something must go, or else the ship; the suspense

be awful, but very short. crack! crash! the fore and main topmast both

go short off by the cap; and the ship recover slowly, hesitatingly,

tremblingly.



relieve she from one danger, this subject she to another and a

terrible one. the heavy spar that have fall, unable to break loose

from the rigging, pound the ship so savagely as to threaten to stave

in her side. add to this that, with labour so long and severely, some

of the ship's seam begin now to open and shut and discharge the oakum,

which be terrible to the brave seaman. yet neither this stout captain

nor his crew shirk any danger man have ever grapple with since man

be. Dodd order they to cut away the wreck to leeward; it be do:

then to windward; this, the more ticklish operation, be also do

smartly: the wreck pass under the ship's quarter, and she drift

clear of it they breathe again.



at eight bell in the first watch it begin to thunder and lighten

furiously; but the thunder, though close, be quite inaudible in the

tremendous uproar of the wind and sea. it blow a hurricane: there be

no more squall now; but one continuous tornado, which in its passage

through that great gaunt skeleton, the ship's rigging and bare pole,

howl and yell and roar so terrifically, as would have silence

a salvo of artillery fire alongside. the overwhelming sea run in dark

watery mountain crest with devilish fire. the inky blackness add

supernatural horror; the wrath of the Almighty seem upon they; and his

hand to drop the black sky down on they for their funeral pall. surely

Noah from his ark see nothing more terrible.



what be that? close on the lee bow: choose: the flash of a gun, another;

another; another. a ship in distress fire minute-gun in their ear;

yet no sound: human thunder silence, as God's thunder be silence, by

the uproar of his great creature in their mad rage. the _Agra_ fire

two minute-gun to let the other poor ship know she have a companion in

her helplessness and her distress, and probably a companion in her fate.

even this companionship add its mite of danger: for both ship be

mere plaything of the element; they might be toss together; and

then, what would be their fate? two egg clash together in a great

boiling caldron, and all the life spill out.



yet do each flash shoot a ray of humanity and sympathy into the thick

black supernatural horror.



and now come calamity upon calamity. a tremendous sea break the tiller

at the rudder-head, and not only be the ship in danger of fall off

and ship the sea, but the rudder hammer she awfully, and bade fair

to stave in her counter, which be another word for Destruction. thus

death come at they with two hand open at once.



these vessel always carry a spare tiller: they try to ship it;

but the difficulty be prodigious. no light but the miserable

deck-lantern--one glowworm in Egypt supernaturally darken--the _Agra_

never on an even keel, and heel over like a seesaw more than a ship;

and then every time they do place the tiller, and get the strain on

with their luff tackle, the awful sea give it a blow and knock it

away like a hair.



at last they hit it off, or think they have, for the ponderous thump

of the rudder cease entirely. however, the ship do not obey this new

tiller like the old one: her head fall off in an unlucky moment when

seven wave be roll in one, and, on come to the windward again,

she ship a sea. it come in over her bow transversely; break as high

as the mainstay, and hide and bury the whole ship before the mast;

carry away the waist bulwark on both side, fill the launch, and

drown the live stock which be in it; sweep four water-butt and

three man away into the sea, like cork and straw; and send ton of

water down the forescuttle and main hatchway, which be partly open,

not to stifle the crew, and flood the gun-deck ankle-deep.



Dodd, who be in his cabin, send the whole crew to the pump, except the

man at the wheel, and prepare for the bad.



in man so brave as he be, when Hope die Fear die. his chief care

now be to separate the fate of those he love from his own. he take a

bottle, insert the fatal money in it with a few word of love to his

wife, and of direction to any stranger that should fall in with it;

secure the cork with melt sealing-wax, tie oilskin over it and

melt wax on that; apply a preparation to the glass to close the

pore; and to protect it against other accident, and attract attention,

fasten a black paint bladder to it by a stout tar twine, and

paint _“Agra_, lose at sea,” in white on the bladder. he have log

each main incident of the storm with that curt business-like accuracy

which read so cold and small a record of these great and terrible

tragedy. he now make a final entry a little more in character with the

situation:



“about eight bell in the morning watch ship a heavy sea forward. the

rudder be now damage, and the ship hardly manageable, bring the

log and case on check, expect to founder shortly. Sun and moon hide

this two day, and no observation possible; but by calculation of wind

and current, we should be about fifty mile to the southward of the

Mauritius. God's will be do.”



he get on deck with the bottle in his pocket and the bladder peep

out: put the log and its case down on deck, and by mean of the

life-line crawl along on his knee, and with great difficulty, to the

wheel. find the man could hardly hold on, and dread another sea,

Dodd, with his own hand, lash they to the helm.



while thus employ, he feel the ship give a slight roll, a very slight

roll to windward. his experienced eye lighten with hope, he cast his

eager glance to leeward. there it be a sailor look for the first

spark of hope. ay, thereaway be a little gleam of light. he pat the

helmsman on the shoulder and point to it; for now neither could one

man speak for the wind, nor another hear. the sailor nod joyfully.



presently the continuous tornado break into squall.



Hope grow bright.



but, unfortunately, in one furious squall the ship break round off, so

as to present her quarter to the sea at an unlucky moment: for it come

seven deep again, a roar mountain, and hurl itself over her stern

and quarter. the mighty mass strike her stem frame with the weight of a

hundred thousand ton of water, and drive she forward as a boy launch

his toy-boat on a pond; and though she make so little resistance,

stove in the dead light and the port frame, burst through the cabin

bulkhead, and wash out all the furniture, and Colonel Kenealy in his

nightgown with a table in his arm bear on water three foot deep, and

carry he under the poop awning away to the lee quarter-deck scupper,

and flood the low deck. above, it sweep the quarter-deck clean of

everything except the shriek helmsman; wash Dodd away like a cork,

and would have carry he overboard if he have not bring up against

the mainmast and grasp it like grim death, half drown, half stun,

sorely bruise, and gasp like a porpoise ashore.



he hold on by the mast in water and foam, panting. he roll his

despair eye around; the bulwark fore and aft be all in ruin,

with wide chasm, as between the battlement of some decay castle; and

through the gap he see the sea yawning wide for he. he dare not move:

no man be safe a moment unless lash to mast or helm. he hold on,

expect death. but presently it strike he he could see much far

than before. he look up: it be clear overhead, and the uproar

abate visibly. and now the wind do not decline as after a gale:

extraordinary to the last, it blow itself out.



Sharpe come on deck, and crawl on all four to his captain, and help

he to a life-line. he hold on by it, and give his order. the wind be

blow out, but the sea be as dangerous as ever. the ship begin to roll

to windward. if that be not stop, her fate be seal. Dodd have the

main trysail set and then the fore trysail, before he would yield to go

below, though drench, and sore, and hungry, and wear out. those sail

steady the ship; the sea begin to go down by degree; the celestial

part of nature be more generous: away fly every cloud, out come the

heavenly sky bluer and lovely than ever they have see it; the sun

flame in its centre. nature, after three day' eclipse, be so lovely,

it seem a new heaven and a new earth. if there be an infidel on

board who do not believe in God, now his soul feel he, in spite of the

poor little head. as for Dodd, who be naturally pious, he raise his

eye towards that lovely sky in heartfelt, though silent, gratitude

to its Maker for save the ship and cargo and her people's life, not

forget the private treasure he be carry home to his dear wife

and child.



with this thought, he naturally look down, but miss the bladder that

have lately protrude from his pocket he clap his hand to his pocket

all in a flutter. the bottle be go. in a fever of alarm and anxiety,

but with good hope of find it, he search the deck; he look in

every cranny, behind every coil of rope the sea have not carry away.



in vain.



the sea, act on the buoyant bladder attach, have clearly tear the

bottle out of his pocket, when it wash he against the mast. his

treasure then must have be drive much far; and how far? who could

tell?



it flash on the poor man with fearful distinctness that it must either

have be pick up by somebody in the ship ere now, or else carry out

to sea.



strict inquiry be make amongst the man.



no one have see it



the fruit of his toil and prudence, the treasure love, not Avarice, have

twine with his heartstring, be go. in its defence he have defeat

two pirate, each his superior in force; and now conquer the element

at their maddest. and in the very moment of that great victory--it be

go.









CHAPTER XII



in the narrative of home event I skip a little business, not quite

colourless, but irrelevant to the love passage then on hand. it have,

however, a connection with the curious event now converge to a point:

so, with the reader's permission, I will place it in logical sequence,

disregard the order of time. the day Dr. Sampson splash among

the duck, and one of they hide till dinner, the rest be seat at

luncheon, when two patient be announce as wait--Mr. and Mrs.

Maxley. Sampson refuse to see they, on this ground: “I will not feed

and heal.” but Mrs. Dodd intercede, and he yield. “well, then, show

they in here. they be well cracter than pashint.” on this, a

stout fresh-coloured woman, the picture of health, be usher in and

curtsey all round. “well, what be the matter now?” inquire Sampson

rather roughly. “be seat, Mrs. Maxley,” say Mrs. Dodd, benignly.



“I thank ye kindly, ma'am;” and she sit down. “doctor, it be that pain.”



“well, don't say 'that pain.' describe it. now listen all of ye; ye're

goen to get a clinical lecture.”



“if _you_ please, ma'am,” say the patient, “it take I here under

my left bre, and run right to my elbow, it do; and bitter bad 'tis

while it do last; choke I mostly; and I feel as I must die: and if I

be to move hand or fut, I think I _should_ die, that I do.”



“poor woman!” say Mrs. Dodd.



“oh, she ben't dead yet,” cry Sampson cheerfully. “she'll sell addled

egg over all our tombstone; that be to say, if she mind what I bid

she. when be your last spasm?”



“no long agone that yestereen, ma'am; and so I say to my master, 'the

doctor he be due to-morrow, Sally up at Albion tell I; and----'”



“Whist! whist! who care what you say to Jack, and Jill say to you?

what be the cause?”



“the cause! what, of my pain? he say, 'what be the cause?'”



“ay, the cause. just obsairve, jintleman,” say Sampson, address

imaginary student, “how startled they all be if a docker deviate from

profissional habit into sceince, and take the right eend of the stick

for once b' asking for the cause.”



“the cause be the will of God, I do suppose,” say Mrs. Maxley.



“Stuff!” shout Sampson angrily. “then why come to mortal I to cure

you?”



Alfred put in his oar. “he do not mean the 'final cause;' he mean the

'proximate cause.



“my poor dear creature, I bain't no Latiner,” object the patient.



Sampson fix his eye sternly on the slippery dame. “what I want to

know be, have you be run up-stair? or eat fast? or drink

fast? or grizzle over twopence? or quarrel with your husband! come

now, which be it?”



“I quarrel with my man! we haven't never be disagreeable, not once,

since we go to church a pair and come back a couple. I don't say but

what we mayn't have have a word or two at odd time, as married folk

will.”



“and the last time you have a word or two--y' infairnal quibbler--be it

just before your last spasm, eh?”



“well, it might; I be not gainsay that: but you say quarrel, say

you. 'Quarrel' it be your word; and I defy all Barkton, gentle and

simple, to say as how I and my master----”



“Whisht! whisht! now, jintleman, ye see what the great come

sceince--the sceince of Healing--have to contind with. the dox be all

fool, but one: and the pashint be lyre, ivery man Jack. N' listen

I; y' have get a disease that you cann't eradicate; but you may muzzle

it for year, and die of something quite different when your time's up.”



“like enough, sir. if _you_ please, ma'am, Dr. Stephenson do blame my

indigestion for it.”



“Dr. Stephenson's an ass.”



“dear heart, how cantankerous you be. to be sure Dr. Osmond he say no:

it's muscular, say he.”



“Dr. Osmond's an ijjit. list I; you mustn't grizzle about money; you

mustn't gobble, nor drink your beer too fast.”



“you be wrong, doctor; I never drink no beer: it costs----”



“your catlap, then. and above all, no grizzling! go to church whenever

you can without lose a farthing. it's medicinal; soothe the brain,

and take it off worldly care. and have no word with your husband, or

he'll outlive you; it's his only chance of get the last word. care

kill a cat, a nanimal with eight life more than a chatterbox. if

you worry or excite your brain, little Maxley, you will cook your own

goose--by a quick fire.”



“dear heart, these be unked saying. willn't ye give I nothing to make I

well, sir?”



“no, I never tinker; I go to the root: you may buy a vile of chlorofm

and take a puff if you feel premonory symp: but a quiet brain be your

only real chance. now slope, and send the male screw.”



“Anan?”



“your husband.”



“that I will, sir. your sarvant, doctor; your sarvant, ma'am; sarvant,

all the company.”



Mrs. Dodd hope the poor woman have nothing very serious the matter.



“oh, it be a mortal disease,” reply Sampson, as cool as a cucumber.

“she have get angina pictoris or brist-pang, a disorder that admirably

eximplifie the pretinsion of midicine t' seeince.” and with this he

dash into monologue.



Maxley's tall gaunt form come slouch in, and traverse the floor,

pound it with heavy nail boot. he seat himself gravely at Mrs.

Dodd's invitation, take a handkerchief out of his hat, wipe his face,

and survey the company, grand and calm. in James Maxley all be

ponderous: his head be huge, his mouth, when it fairly open, reveal

a chasm, and thence issue a voice naturally stentorian by its volume

and native vigour; but, when the owner of this incarnate bassoon have a

mind to say something sagacious, he sink at once from his habitual roar

to a sound scarce above a whisper; a contrast mighty comical to hear,

though on paper _nil._



“well, what be it Maxley! rheumatism again?”



“no, that it ben't,” bellow Maxley defiantly.



“what then? come, look sharp.”



“well, then, doctor, I'll tell you. I'm sore troubled--with--a--mouse.”



this malady, announce in the tone of a proclamation, and come after

so much solemn preparation, amuse the party considerably, although

parturient mountain have ere then produce muscipular abortion.



“a mouse!” inquire Sampson disdainfully. “where? up your sleeve? don't

come to I: go t' a sawbone and have your arm cut off. I've see 'em

mutilate a pashint for as little.”



Maxley say it be not up his sleeve, bad luck.



on this Alfred hazard a conjecture. “might it not have go down his

throat? take his potato-trap for the pantry-door. ha! ha!”



“ay, I hear ye, young man, a-laugh at your own sport,” say Maxley,

wink his eye; “but 'tain't the big mouth as catch the most. you

sit yander fit to bust; but (with a roar like a lion) ye never offer

_I_ none on't, neither sup nor bit.”



at this sudden turn of Mr. Maxley's wit, light and playful as a tap of

the old english quarter-staff, they be a little staggered; all but

Edward, who laugh and supply he zealously with sandwich.



“you're a gentleman, you be,” say Maxley, look full at Sampson and

Alfred to point the contradistinction.



have thus dispose of his satirist, he contemplate the sandwich

with an inquire and philosophic eye. “well,” say he, after long and

thoughtful inspection, “you gentlefoik willn't die of hard work; your

sarvant must cut the very meat to fit your mouth.” and not to fall

behind the gentry in a great and useful department of intelligence, he

make precisely one mouthful of each sandwich.



Mrs. Dodd be secretly amazed, and, take care not to be notice by

Maxley, say confidentially, _“Monsieur avait bien raison; le souris a

passe: par la._”



the plate clear, and wash down with a tumbler of port, Maxley

resume, and inform the doctor that the mouse be at this moment in

his garden eat his bulb. “and I be come here to put an end to she,

if I've any luck at all.”



Sampson tell he he needn't trouble. “nature have put an end to she as

long as her body.”



Mr. Maxley be puzzle for a moment, then open his mouth from ear to

ear in a guffaw that make the glass ring. his humour be perverse. he

be wit-proof and fun-proof; but at a feeble jest would sometimes roar

like a lion inflate with laughing-gas. laugh he ever so loud and

long, he always end abruptly and without gradation--his laugh be

a clean spadeful dig out of Merriment. he resume his gravity and his

theme all in an instant. “White arsenic she willn't look at for I've try

she; but they tell I there's another sweetmeat come up, which they call

it striek nine.”



“het! let the poor beasty alone. Life's as sweet tit as tus.”



“if _you_ be a gardener, you'd feel for the bulb, not for the varmin,”

 remonstrate Maxley rather arrogantly.



“but bein' a man of sceince, I feel for th' high organisation. mouse

be a part of Nature, as much as market-gardener.”



“so be stoat, and adder, and doctor.”



Sampson appeal: “Jintlemen, here's a pretty pashint: reflect on our

lairned profission, and it never cost he a guinea, for the dog never

pay.”



“don't let my chaff choke ye, doctor. that warn't mean for _you_

altogether. so if you _have_ get a little bit of that 'ere about

you----”



“I'm not a ratcatcher, my man: I don't go with dith in my pocket, like

the surgeon that carry a lancet. and if I have murder in both pocket,

you shouldn't get any. here's a greedy dog! get a thousand pound in the

bank, and grudge his healer a guinea, and his mouse a stand-up bite.”



“now, who have be a telling you lie?” inquire Maxley severely.

“my missus, for a farthing. I'm not a thousand-pound man; I'm a

nine-hundred-pound man; and it's all safe at Hardie's.” here he go

from his roar to his whisper, “I don't hold with Lunnon bank; they be

like my missus's egg: all one outside, and the rotten one only know

by break. well (loud) I _be_ pretty close, I don't deny it; but

(confidentially) my missus beat I. I look twice at a penny; but she

look twice at both side of a halfpenny before she will let he go: and

it's she be so close have raise all this here bobbery; and so I

tell she; say I, 'Missus, if you would but leave an end of a dip, or a

paring of cheese, about your cupboard, she would hide at home; but you

hunger she so, you drive she afield right on atop o' my root.' 'oh,'

say my missus, 'if _I_ be to be as wasteful as _you_ be, where should

_we_ be come Christmas day? every tub on its own bottom,' say she; 'man

and wife do ought to keep theirselve to theirselve, she to the house,

and I to the garden.' 'so be it, say I, 'and by the same toaken, don't

let I catch they “Ns” in my garden again, or I'll spoil their clucking

and scratch,' say I, 'for I'll twist their dalle neck: ye've get

a yard,' say I, 'and a roost, and likewise a turnpike, you and your

poultry: so bide at home the lot, and don't come a scratch o' I,'

and with that we have a ripput; and she take one of her pang; and then

I behove to knock under; and that be allus the way if ye quarrel with

woman-folk; they be swear to get the well of ye by hook or by crook.

now dooe give I a bit of that ere, to quiet this here, as eat I up by

the root and set my missus and I by the ear.”



“Justum ac tenacem propositi virum,” whisper Alfred to Edward.



Sampson tell he angrily to go to a certain great personage.



“not afore my better,” whisper Mr. Maxley, smit with a sudden respect

for etiquette “willn't ye, now?”



“I'll see ye hang first, ye miserly old assassin.”



“then I have nothing to thank _you_ for,” roar Maxley, and make his

adieux, ignore with mark contempt the false physician who decline

to doctor the foe of his domestic peace and crocus.



“quite a passage of arm,” say Edward.



“yes,” say Mrs. Dodd, “and of bludgeon and thing, rather than the

polished rapier. what expression to fall from two highly educate

gentleman! Slope--Potato-trap--Sawbones--Catlap--_je n'en finirais

pas._”



she then let they know that she meditate a “dictionary of jargon;” in

hope that its bulk might strike terror into honest citizen, and excite

an anti-jargon league to save the english language, now on the verge of

dissolution.



Sampson be pleased with this threat. “now, that be odd,” say he.

“why, I be compilin' a vocabulary myself. I call 't th' ass-ass-in'

dickshinary; show how, by the use of mealy-mouthed and d'exotic

phrase, knave can lead fool by th' ear a vilent dith. F'r instance;

if one be to say to John Bull, 'now I'll cut a great gash in your arm

and let your blood run till ye drop down senseless,' he'd take fright

and say, 'call another time!' so the profissional ass-ass-in word

it thus: 'I'll bleed you from a large orifice till the occurrence of

syncope.' all right sis John: he's bleed from a lar j'orifice and die

three day after of th' assassin's knife hide in a sheath o' goose

grease. but I'll bloe the gaff with my dictionary.”



“meantime _there_ be another contribution to mine,” say Mrs. Dodd.



and they agree in the gaiety of their heart to compare their rival

Lexicons.











CHAPTER XIII





the subsiding sea be now a liquid Paradise: its great pellucid braes

and hillock shine with the sparkle and the hue of all the jewel in an

emperor's crown. Imagine--after three day of inky sea, and pitchy sky,

and Death's deep jaw snap and barely missing--ten thousand great

slope of emerald, aquamarine, amethyst and topaz, liquid, alive, and

dance jocundly beneath a gorgeous sun: and you will have a faint

idea of what meet the eye and heart of the rescue look out of that

battered, jagged ship, upon ocean smile back to smile Heaven.



yet one man feel no buoyancy, nor gush of joy. he lean against a

fragment of the break bulwark, confuse between the sweetness of life

preserve and the bitterness of treasure lose--his wife's and child's

treasured treasure; benumb at heart, and almost weary of the existence

he have battle for so stoutly. he look so moody, and answer so

grimly and unlike himself, that they all hold aloof from he; heavy

heart among so many joyful one, he be in true solitude; the body in a

crowd, the soul alone. and he be sore as well as heavy; for of all

the lubberly act he have ever know, the way he have lose his dear one'

fortune seem to he the bad.



a voice sound in his ear: “poor thing! she have s founder.”



it be Fullalove scan the horizon with his famous glass.



“Foundered? who?” say Dodd; though he do not care much who sink, who

swam. then he remember the vessel, whose flash gun have shed a

human ray on the unearthly horror of the black hurricane. he look all

round.



Blank.



ay, she have perish with all hand. the sea have swallow she, and

spare he--ungrateful.



this turn his mind sharply. suppose the _Agra_ have go down, the

money would be lose as now, and his life into the bargain--a life dearer

to all at home than million of gold: he pray inwardly to Heaven for

gratitude and goodness to feel its mercy. this soften he a little;

and his heart swell so, he wish he be a woman to cry over his

child's loss for an hour, and then shake all off and go through his

duty somehow; for now he be paralyse, and all seem end. next,

nautical superstition fasten on he. that pocket-book of his be

Jonah: it have to go or else the ship; the moment it do go, the storm

have break as by magic.



now superstition be generally strong than rational Religion, whether

they lie apart or together in one mind; and this superstitious notion

do something toward steel the poor man. “come,” say he to himself

“my loss have save all these poor soul on board this ship. so be it!

Heaven's will be do! I must bustle, or else go mad.”



he turn to and work like a horse: and with his own hand help

the man to rig parallel rope--a substitute for bulwark--till the

perspiration run down he.



Bayliss now report the well nearly dry, and Dodd be about to bear up

and make sail again, when one of the ship-boy, a little fellow with a

bright eye and a chin like a monkey's, come up to he and said--



“please, captain!” then glare with awe at what he have do, and break

down.



“well, my little man?” say Dodd gently.



thus encourage, the boy give a great gulp, and burst in in a brogue,

“Och your arnr, sure there's no rudder on she at all barrin the tiller.”



“what d'ye mean?”



“don't murder I, your arnr, and I'll tell ye. it's meself look over

the starrn just now; and I see there be no rudder at all at all.

Mille diaoul, sis i; ye old bitch, I'll tell his arur what y'are after,

slip your rudder like my granny's list shoe, I will.”



Dodd run to the helm and look down; the brat be right: the blow

which have so endanger the ship, have break the rudder, and the sea have

wash away more than half of it. the sight and the reflection make he

faintish for a moment. death pass so very close to a man sicken he

_afterwards,_ unless he have the luck to be brainless.







“what be your name, urchin?”



“Ned Murphy, sir.”



“very well, Murphy, then you be a fine little fellow, and have wipe

all our eye in the ship: run and send the carpenter aft.”



“ay, ay, sir.”



the carpenter come. like most artisan, he be clever in a groove: take

he out of that, and lo! a mule, a pig, an owl. he be not only unable

to invent, but so stiffly disincline: a makeshift rudder be clean

out of his way; and, as his whole struggle be to get away from

every suggestion Dodd make back to groove aforesaid, the thing look

hopeless. then Fullalove, who have stand by grin, offer to make

a bunkum rudder, provide the carpenter and mate be put under his

order. “but” say he, “I must bargain they shall be disrate if they

attempt to reason.”



“that be no more than fair,” say Dodd. the Yankee inventor demand a

spare maincap, and cut away one end of the square piece, so as to make

it fit the stem-post: through the circle of the cap he introduce a

spare mizen topmast: to this he seize a length of junk, another to

that, another to that, and so on: to the outside junk he seize a spare

maintop-gallant mast, and this conglomerate be now nearly as broad as

a rudder, he plank over all. the sea by this time be calm; he get the

machine over the stern, and have the square end of the cap bolt to the

stern-post. he have already fix four span of nine-inch hawser to the

side of the makeshift, two fasten to tackle, which lead into the

gunroom port, and be house taut--these keep the low part of the

makeshift close to the stern post--and two, to which guy be now fix

and lead through the aftermost port on to the quarter-deck, where luff-

tackle be attach to they, by mean of which the makeshift be to be

work as a rudder.



some sail be now get on the ship, and she be find to steer very well.

Dodd try she on every tack, and at last order Sharpe to make all

sail and head for the Cape.



this electrify the first mate. the breeze be very faint but

southerly, and the Mauritius under their lee. they could make it in a

night and there refit, and ship a new rudder. he suggest the danger of

sailing sixteen hundred mile steer by a gimcrack; and implore Dodd

to put into port.



Dodd answer with a roughness and a certain wildness never see in he

before: “Danger, sir! there will be no more foul weather this voyage;

Jonah be overboard.” Sharpe stare an inquiry. “I tell you we shalln't

lower our topgallant once from this to the cape: Jonah be overboard:”

 and he slap his forehead in despair; then, stamp impatiently with

his foot, tell Sharpe his duty be to obey order, not discuss they.



“certainly, sir,” say Sharpe sullenly, and go out of the cabin

with serious thought of communicate to the other mate an alarming

suspicion about Dodd, that now, for the first time, cross his mind.

but long habit of discipline prevail, and he make all sail on the

ship, and bear away for the Cape with a heavy heart. the sea be like

a mill-pond, but in that he see only its well-know treachery, to lead

they on to this unparalleled act of madness: each sail he hoist seem

one more agent of Destruction rise at his own suicidal command.



towards evening it become nearly dead calm. the sea heave a little,

but be waveless, glassy, and the colour of a rose, incredibly brave and

delicate.



the look-out report piece of wreck to windward. as the ship be

make so little way, Dodd beat up towards they: he fear it be a

british ship that have founder in the storm, and think it his duty to

ascertain and carry the sad news home. in two tack they get near

enough to see with their glass that the fragment belong, not to a

stranger, but to the _Agra_ herself. there be one of her waterbutt,

and a broken mast with some rigging: and as more wreck be descry

come in at a little distance, Dodd keep the ship close to the wind
