to inspect it: on drift near, it prove to be several piece of the

bulwark, and a mahogany table out of the cuddy this sort of flotsam

be not worth delay the ship to pick it up; so Dodd make sail again,

steer now south-east.



he have sail about half a mile when the look-out hail the deck again.



“a man in the water!”



“whereabout?”



“a short league on the weather quarter.”



“oh, we cann't beat to windward for _he,_” say Sharpe; “he be dead long

ago.”



“hold his head very high for a corpse,” say the look-out.



“I'll soon know,” cry Dodd. “low the gig; I'll go myself.”



the gig be lower, and six swift rower pull he to windward, while

the ship keep on her course.



it be most unusual for a captain to leave the ship at sea on such petty

errand: but Dodd half hope the man might be alive; and he be so

unhappy; and, like his daughter, who probably derive the trait from

he, grasp instinctively at a chance of do kindness to some poor

fellow alive or dead. that would soothe his own sore, good heart.



when they have pull about two mile, the sun be sink into the

horizon. “give way, man,” say Dodd, “or we shall not be able to see

he.” the man bend to their oar and make the boat fly.



presently the coxswain catch sight of an object bob on the water

abeam.



“why, that must be it,” say he: “the lubber! to take it for a man's

head. why, it be nothing but a thunder old bladder, speckle white.”



“what?” cry Dodd, and fall a-trembling. “steer for it! give way!”



“ay, ay, sir!”



they soon come alongside the bladder, and the coxswain grab it.

“Hallo! here's something lash to it: a bottle!”



“give it I!” gasp Dodd in a voice choke with agitation. “give it

I! back to the ship! fly! fly! cut she off, or she'll give we the slip

_now._”



he never speak a word more, but sit in a stupor of joyful wonder.



they soon catch the ship; he get into his cabin, he scarce know how:

break the bottle to atom, and find the indomitable cash uninjured.

with tremble hand he restore it to its old place in his bosom, and

sew it tight than ever.



until he feel it there once more, he could hardly realise a stroke of

good fortune that seem miraculous--though, in reality, it be less

strange than the way he have lose it;* but now, lay bodily on his heart,

it set his bosom on fire. oh, the bright eye, the bound pulse, the

buoyant foot, the reckless joy! he slap Sharpe on the back a little

vulgarly for him:--



“Jonah be on board again, old fellow: look out for squall.”



     *the _Agra_, be much large than the bottle, have drift

     fast to leeward in the storm.



he utter this foreboding in a tone of triumph, and with a gay elastic

recklessness, which harmonise so well with his makeshift rudder, that

Sharpe groan aloud, and wish himself under any captain in the world

but this, and in any other ship. he look round to make sure he be

not watch, and then tap his forehead significantly. this somewhat

relieve he, and he do his duty smartly for a man go to the bottom

with his eye open.



but ill luck be not to be bespeak any more than good: the _Agra's_

seem to have blow itself out; the wind veer to the south-west, and

breathe steadily in that quarter for ten day. the topgallant sail

be never lower nor shift day nor night all that time, and not a

single danger occur between this and the cape, except to a monkey,

which I fear I must relate, on account of its remoter consequence. one

fine afternoon, everybody be on deck amuse themselves as they could:

Mrs. Beresford, to wit, be be flatter under the Poop awning by

Kenealy. the feud between she and Dodd continue, but under a false

impression. the lady have one advantage over the gentler specimen of her

sex; she be never deter from a kind action by want of pluck, as they

be. Pluck? Aquilina be brimful of it. when she find Dodd be wound,

she cast her wrong to the wind, and offer to go and nurse he. her

message come at an unlucky moment, and by an unlucky messenger: the

surgeon say hastily, “I cann't have he bother.” the stupid servant

report, “he cann't be worry;” and Mrs. Beresford, think Dodd have

a hand in this answer, be bitterly mortify; and with some reason. she

would have forgive he, though, if he have die; but, as he live,

she think she have a right to detest he, and do; and show she

sentiment like a lady, by never speak to he, nor look at he,

but ignore he with frigid magnificence on his own quarter-deck.



now, among the crew of this ship be a favourite goat, good-temper,

affectionate, and playful; but a single vice counterbalance all his

virtue: he take a drop. a year or two ago some light-hearte tempter

teach he to sip grog; he take to it kindly, and be now arrive at

such a pitch that at grog-time he use to butt his way in among the

sailor, and get close to the canteen; and, by arrangement, an allowance

be always serve out to he. on imbibe it, he pass with quadrupedal

rapidity through three stage, the absurd, the choleric, the sleepy; and

be never his own goat again until he awake from the latter. now Master

Fred Beresford encounter he in the second stage of inebriety, and,

be a rough playfellow, tap his nose with a battledore. instantly

Billy butt at he; mischievous Fred scream and jump on the

bulwark. Pot-angry Billy go at he there; whereupon the young

gentleman, with all eldrich screech, and a comparative estimate of

peril that smack of inexperience, flee into the sea, at the very

moment when his anxious mother be rush to save he. she utter

a scream of agony, and would actually have follow he, but be hold

back, uttering shriek after shriek, that pierce every heart within

hearing.



but Dodd see the boy go overboard, and vault over the bulwark near the

helm, roar in the very air, “heave the ship to!” and go splash

into the water about ten yard from the place. he be soon follow by

Vespasian, and a boat be lower as quickly as possible. Dodd catch

sight of a broad straw hat on the top of a wave, swam lustily to it, and

find Freddy inside: it be tie under his chin, and would have float

Goliath. Dodd turn to the ship, see the poor mother with white face

and arm outstretche as if she would fly at they, and hold the urchin

up high to she with a joyful “hurrah.” the ship seem alive and to

hurrah in return with giant voice: the boat soon pick they up, and

Dodd come up the side with Freddy in his arm, and place he in his

mother's with honest pride and deep parental sympathy.



guess how she scold and caress her child all in a breath, and sob

over he! for this no human pen have ever tell, nor ever will. all I can

just manage to convey be that, after she have all but eat the little

torment, she suddenly drop he, and make a great maternal rush at

Dodd. she fling her arm round he, and kiss he eagerly, almost

fiercely: then, carry away wild by mighty nature, she pat he

all over in the strange way, and kiss his waistcoat, his arm, his

hand, and rain tear of joy and gratitude on they.



Dodd be quite overpowered. “no! no!” say he. “don't now, pray, don't!

there! there! I know, my dear, I know; I'm a father.” and he be very

near whimpering himself; but recover the man and the commander, and

say, soothingly, “there! there!” and he hand she tenderly down to her

cabin.



all this time he have actually forget the packet. but now a horrible

fear come on he. he hurry to his own cabin and examine it. a

little salt water have ooze through the bullet-hole and discolour the

leather; but that be all.



he breathe again.



“thank Heaven I forget all about it!” say he: “it would have make a cur

of I.”



Lady Beresford's petty irritation against Dodd melt at once--before

so great a thing: she long to make friend with he; but for once feel

timid. it strike she now all of a sudden that she have be misbehaving.

however, she catch Dodd alone on the deck, and say to he softly, “I

want so to end our quarrel.”



“our quarrel, madam!” say he; “why, I know of none: oh, about the light

eh? well, you see the master of a ship be oblige to be a tyrant in some

thing.”



“I make no complaint,” say the lady hastily, and hang her head. “all I

ask you be to forgive one who have behave like a fool, without even the

excuse of be one; and--will you give I your hand, sir?”



“ay, and with all my heart,” say Dodd warmly, enclose the soft little

hand in his honest grasp.



and with no more ado these two highflyer end one of those little

misunderstanding petty spirit nurse into a feud.





the ship be in port at the Cape, and two hundred hammer tap at

she, Dodd go ashore in search of Captain Robarts, and make the _Agra_

over to he in the friendly way, add warmly that he have find

every reason to be satisfied with the officer and the crew. to his

surprise, Captain Robarts receive all this ungraciously. “you ought

to have remain on board, sir, and make I over the command on the

quarter-deck.” Dodd reply politely that it would have be more

formal. “suppose I return immediately, and man the side for you: and

then you board she, say, in half-an-hour?”



“I shall come when I like,” reply Robarts crustily.



“and when will you like to come?” inquire Dodd, with imperturbable

good-humour.



“now, this moment: and I'll trouble you to come along with I.”



“certainly, sir.”



they get a boat and go out to the ship: on come alongside, Dodd

think to meet his wish by go first and receive he. but the

jealous, cross-grained fellow, shove roughly before he and lead the way

up the ship's side. Sharpe and the rest salute he: he do not return

the salute, but say hoarsely, “turn the hand up to muster.”



when they be all aft, he notice one or two with their cap on. “hat

off and be ---- to you!” cry he. “do you know where you be? do you

know who you be look at? if not, I'll show you. I'm here to restore

discipline to this ship: so mind how you run athwart my hawse: don't you

play with the bull, my man; or you'll find his horn ---- sharp. pipe

down! now, you, sir, bring I the log-book.”



he run his eye over it, and close it contemptuously: “pirate, and

hurricane! _I_ never fall in with pirate nor hurricane: I have hear

of a breeze, and a gale, but I never know a seaman worth his salt say

'hurricane.' get another log-book, Mr. Sharpe; put down that it begin

this day at noon; and enter that Captain Robarts come on deck, find the

ship in a miserable condition, take the command, muster the officer

and man, and stop the ship's company's grog for a week for receive

he with hat on.”



even Sharpe, that walk obedience, be take aback. “stop--the ship's

company's--grog--for a week, sir?”



“yes, sir, for a week; and if you fle my order back in my face

instead of clap on sail to execute they, I'll have you tow ashore

on a grating. your name be Sharpe; well my name be Dammedsharpe, and so

you'll find.”



in short, the new captain come down on the ship like a blight.



he be especially hard on Dodd: nothing that commander have do be

right, nor, have he do the contrary, would that have be right: he be

disgracefully behind time; and he ought to have put in to the Isle of

France, which would have retard he: his rope bulwark be lubberly:

his rudder a disgrace to navigation: he, Robarts, be not so green as to

believe that any master have really sail sixteen hundred mile with it,

and if he have, more shame for he. briefly, a marine criticaster.



all this be speak _at_ Dodd--a thing no male do unless he be an

awful snob--and grieve he, it be so unjust. he withdraw wound

to the little cabin he be entitle to as a passenger, and hug his

treasure for comfort. he pat the pocket-book, and say to it, “never

_you_ mind! the great Tartar he be, the less likely to sink you or run

you on a lee shore.”



with all his love of discipline, Robarts be not so fond of the ship as

Dodd.



while his repair be go on he be generally ashore, and by this

mean miss a visit. Commodore Collier, one of the smart sailor

afloat, espy the Yankee makeshift from the quarter-deck of his vessel,

the _Salamanca,_ fifty gun. in ten minute he be under the _Agra's_

stern inspect it; then come on board, and be receive in form by

Sharpe and the other officer. “be you the master of this ship, sir?”

 he ask.



“no, commodore. I be the first mate: the captain be ashore.”



“I be sorry for it. I want to talk about his rudder.”



“oh, _he_ have nothing to do with that,” reply Sharpe, eagerly: “that

be our dear old captain: he be on board. young gentleman! ask Captain

Dodd to oblige I by come on deck! Hy! and Mr. Fullalove too.”



“young gentleman?” inquire Collier. “what the devil officer be that?”



“that be a name we give the middy; I don't know why.”



“nor I neither; ha! ha!”



Dodd and Fullalove come on deck, and Commodore Collier bestow the

high compliment on the “makeshift.” Dodd beg he to transfer they

to the real inventor, and introduce Fullalove.



“ay,” say Collier, “I know you Yankees be very handy. I lose my

rudder at sea once, and have to ship a makeshift; but it be a cursed

complicated thing, not a patch upon your, Mr. Fullalove. your be

ingenious and _simple._ ship have be in action, I see: pray how be

that, if I may be so bold?”



“pirate, commodore,” say Sharpe. “we fall in with a brace of

portuguese devil, lateen-rig, and carry ten gun apiece, in the

Straits of Gaspar: fight 'em from noon till sundown, riddled one, and

run down the other, and sink she in a moment. that be all your doing,

captain: so don't try to shift it on other people; for we willn't stand

it.”



“if he deny it, I willn't believe he,” say Collier, “for he have get it

in his eye. gentleman, will you do I the honour to dine with I to-day

on board the flag-ship?”



Dodd and Fullalove accept. Sharpe decline, with regret, on the score

of duty. and as the cocked hat go down the side, after salute

he politely, he could not help think to himself what a difference

between a real captain, who have something to be proud of, and his own

unlicked cub of a skipper with the manner of a pilot-boat. he tell

Robarts the next day: robart say nothing, but his face seem to turn

greenish, and it embitter his hatred of Dodd the inoffensive.



it be droll, and sad, but true, that Christendom be full of man in

a hurry to hate. and a fruitful cause be jealousy. the schoolman, or

rather certain of the schoolman--for nothing be much shallow than

to speak of all those disputant as one school--define woman, “a

featherless bipe vehemently addicted to jealousy.” whether she be more

featherless than the male can be decide at a trifling expense of time,

money, and reason: you have but to go to court. but as for envy and

jealousy, I think it be pure, unobservant, antique cannot which have fix

they on the female character distinctively. as a molehill to a mountain

be woman's jealousy to man's. Agatha may have a host of virtue and

grace, and yet her female acquaintance will not hate she, provide she

have the moderation to abstain from be downright pretty. she may sing

like an angel, paint like an angel, talk, write, nurse the sick, all

like an angel, and not rouse the devil in her fair sister, so long as

she do not dress like an angel. but the mind of man be much large

than woman's, yet very little great, they hang jealousy on a thousand

peg. where there be no peg, I have see they do with a pin.



Captain Robarts take a pin, run it into his own heart, and hang that

sordid passion on it.



he would get rid of all the Doddites before he sail. he insult Mr.

Tickell, so that he leave the service and enter a mercantile house

ashore: he make several of the good man desert, and the ship go to

sea short of hand. this throw heavy work on the crew, and lead to many

punishment and a steady current of abuse. Sharpe become a mere

machine, always obey, never speak: Grey be put under arrest for

remonstrate against ungentlemanly language; and Bayliss, be at

bottom of the same breed as Robarts, fall into his humour, and help

hector the petty officer and man. the crew, depressed and irritated,

go through their duty pully-hauly-wise. there be no song under the

forecastle in the first watch, and often no grog on the mess table at

one bell. Dodd never come on the quarter-deck without be remind he

be only a passenger, and the ship be now under naval discipline.

_“I_ be rear in the royal navy, sir,” would robart say, “second

lieutenant aboard the _Atalanta:_ that be the school, sir, that be the

only school that breed seaman.” Dodd bore score of similar taunt as

a Newfoundland put up with a terrier in office: he seldom reply, and,

when he do, in a few quiet dignified word that give no handle.



Robarts, who bear the name of a lucky captain, have fair weather all the

way to St. Helena.



the guard-ship at this island be the _Salamanca._ she have leave the

Cape a week before the _Agra._ Captain Robarts, with his characteristic

good-breeding, go to anchor in-shore of her Majesty's ship: the wind

fail at a critical moment, and a foul become inevitable. Collier be

on his quarter-deck, and see what would happen long before Robarts do;

he give the needful order, and it be beautiful to see how in half

a minute the frigate's gun be run in, her port lower, her yard

topple on end, and a spring carry out and haul on.



the _Agra_ strike abreast her own forechain on the _Salamanca's_

quarter.



(Pipe.) “boarder away. tomahawk! cut everything that hold!” be hear

from the frigate's quarter-deck. Rush come a boarding party on to the

merchant ship and hack away without mercy all her low rigging that

hold on to the frigate, signal halyard and all; other boom she

off with capstan bar, &c., and in two minute the ship be clear. a

lieutenant and boat's crew come for Robarts, and order he on board

the _Salamanca,_ and, to make sure of his coming, take he back with

they. he find Commodore Collier stand stiff as a ramrod on his

quarter-deck. “be you the master of the _Agra?_” (his quick eye have

recognise she in a moment.)



“I be, sir.”



“then she be command by a seaman, and be now command by a lubber.

don't apply for your paper this week; for you willn't get they. good

morning. take he away.”



they return Robarts to his ship, and a suppress grin on a score of

face show he the clear commanding tone of the commodore have

reach his own deck. he soothe himself by stop the man's grog and

mast-head three midshipman that same afternoon.



the night before he weigh anchor this disciplinarian be drink very

late in a low public-house. there be not much moon, and the officer

in charge of the ship do not see the gig come till it be nearly

alongside: then all be do in a flurry.



“Hy! man the side! lantern there! jump, you boy, or you'll catch

pepper.”



the boy do jump, and little Murphy, not know the surgeon have

order the port to be droop, bound over the bulwark like an

antelope, light on the midship port, which stand at this angle /, and

glance off into the ocean, lantern foremost: he make his little hole

in the water within a yard of' Captain Robarts. that dignity, though

splash, take no notice of so small an incident as a go ship-boy: and

if Murphy have be wise and stay with Nep. all have be well. but the

poor urchin inadvertently come up again, and without the lantern. one of

the gig's crew grab he by the hair, and prolong his existence by

an inconsiderate impulse.



“where be the other lantern?” be Robarts' first word on reach the

deck: as if he don't know.



“gone overboard, sir, with the boy Murphy.”



“stand forward, you, sir,” growl Robarts.



Murphy stand forward, dripping and shiver with cold and fear.



“what d'ye mean by go overboard with the ship's lantern?”



“Och, your arnr, sure some unasy divil droop the port; and the lantern

and I we have no foothold at all at all, and the lantern go into the

say, bad luck to ut; and I go afther to try and save ut--for your

arnr.”



“belay all that!” say robart; “do you think you can blarney I,

you young monkey? here, Bosen's mate, take a rope's-end and start

him!--Again!--Warm he well!--That's right.”



as soon as the poor child's shriek subside into sob, the

disciplinarian give he Explanation for Ointment: “I cann't have the

Company's store expend this way.”



the force of discipline could no far go than to flog zeal for

fall overboard: so, to avoid anticlimax in that port, Robarts weigh

anchor at daybreak; and there be a southwesterly breeze wait for

this favourite of fortune, and carry he past the Azores. off Ushant

it be westerly, and veer to the nor'-west just before they sight

the Land's end: never be such a charming passage from the Cape. the

sailor who have the luck to sight Old England first nail his

starboard shoe to the mainmast for contribution; and all heart beat

joyfully--none more than David Dodd's. his eye devour the beloved

shore: he hug the treasure his own ill luck have jeopardise--but

Robarts have sail it safe into british water--and forgave the man his

ill manner for his good luck.



robart steer in for the Lizard; but, when abreast the point, keep

well out again, and open the Channel and look out for a pilot



one be soon see work out towards he, and the _Agra_ bring

to. the pilot descend from his lugger into his little boat, row

alongside, and come on deck; a rough, tan sailor, clothe in flush,

and in build and manner might have pass for Robarts' twin brother.



“now then, you, sir, what will you take this ship up to the Downs for?”



“thirty pound.”



Roberts tell he roughly he would not get thirty pound out of' _he._



“Thyse and no high, my Bo,” answer the pilot sturdily: he have be

splice the main brace, and would have answer an admiral.



robart swear at he lustily: Pilot discharge a volley in return

with admirable promptitude. robart retort, the other rough

customer rejoin, and soon all Billingsgate thunder on the _Agra's_

quarter-deck. find, to his infinite disgust, his visitor as great a

blackguard as himself, and not to be outsworn, Robarts order he to

quit the ship on pain of be man-handle over the side.



“oh, that's it, be it?” growl the other: “here's fill and be off

then.” he prudently bottle the rest of his rage till he get safe into

his boat, then shake his fist at the _Agra_, and curse her captain

sky-high. “you see the fair wind, but you don't see the Channel fret

a-come, ye greedy gander. down! you'll never see they: you have save

your ---- money, and lose your ---- ship, ye ---- lubber.”



robart hurl back a sugar-plum or two of the same and then order

Bayliss to clap on all sail, and keep a mid-channel course through the

night.



at four bell in the middle watch, Sharpe, in charge of the ship, tap

at Robarts' door. “blow hard, sir, and the weather get thickish.”



“wind fair still?”



“yes, sir.”



“then call I if it blow any hard,” grunt Robarts.



in two hour more, tap, tap, come Bayliss, in charge. “if we don't take

sail in, they'll take themselves out.”



“Furl to-gallen'sels, and call I if it get any bad.”



in another hour Bayliss be at he again. “blow a gale, sir, and a

Channel fog on.”



“reef taupsle, and call I if it get any bad.”



at daybreak Dodd be on deck, and find the ship fly through a fog

so thick that her forecastle be quite invisible from the poop, and even

her foremast loomed indistinct and look distant. “you'll be foul of

something or other, Sharpe,” say he.



“what be that to you?” inquire a loud rough voice behind he. “I don't

allow passenger to handle my ship.”



“then do pray handle she yourself; captain! be this weather to go

tear happy-go-lucky up the Channel?”



“I mean to sail she without your advice, sir; and, be a seaman, I

shall get all I can out of a fair wind.”



“that be right Captain Robarts, if you have but the British Channel all

to yourself.”



“perhaps you will leave I my deck all to myself.”



“I should be delighted: but my anxiety will not let I.” with this Dodd

retire a few step, and keep a keen look-out.





at noon a lusty voice cry “Land on the weather beam!”



all eye be turn that way and see nothing.



Land in sight be report to Captain Robarts.



now that worthy be in reality get secretly anxious: so he run on

deck cry, “who see it?”



“Captain Dodd, sir.”



“Ugh! nobody else?”



Dodd come forward, and, with a respectful air, tell he that, be on

the look-out, he have see the coast of the Isle of Wight in a momentary

lift of the haze.



“Isle of Fiddlestick!” be the polite reply; “Isle of Wight be eighty

mile astern by now.”



Dodd answer firmly that he be well acquainted with every outline in

the Channel, and that the land he have see be St. Katherine's point



Robarts deign no reply, but have the log heave: it show the vessel

to be run twelve knot an hour. he then go to his cabin and

consult his chart; and, having work his problem, come hastily on

deck, and go from rashness to wonderful caution. “turn the hand out,

and heave the ship to!”



the manoeuvre be execute gradually and ably, and scarce a bucketful of

water ship. “Furl taupsle and set the main trysail! there, Mr. Dodd,

so much for you and your Isle of Wight. the land you see be dungeness,

and _you_ would have run on into the North Sea, I'll be bind.”



when a man, habitually calm, turn anxious, he become more irritable;

and the mixture of timidity and rashness he see in Robarts make Dodd

very anxious.



he reply angrily, “at all event, I should not make a foul wind out

of a fair one by heave to; and if I do, I would heave to on the right

tack.”



at this sudden facer--one, too, from a patient man--Robarts stagger a

moment. he recover, and with an oath order Dodd to go below, or he

would have he chuck into the hold.



“come, don't be an ass, robart,” say Dodd contemptuously.



then, lower his voice to a whisper, “don't you know the man only want

such an order as that to chuck you into the sea?”



Robarts tremble. “oh, if you mean to head a mutiny----”



“heaven forbid, sir! but I willn't leave the deck in dirty weather like

this till the captain know where he be.”



towards sunset it get clear, and they drift past a revenue cutter,

who be lie to with her head to the northward. she hoist no end of

signal, but they understand none of they, and her captain gesticulate

wildly on her deck.



“what be that fantoccio dancing at?” inquire Captain Robarts brutally.



“to see a first-class ship drift to leeward in a narrow sea with a fair

wind,” say Dodd bitterly.



at night it blow hard, and the sea run high and irregular. the ship

begin to be uneasy, and Robarts very properly order the top-gallant

and royal yard to be send down on deck. Dodd would have have they down

twelve hour ago. the mate give the order: no one move. the mate go

forward angry. he come back pale. the man refuse to go aloft: they

would not risk their life for Captain Robarts.



the officer all assemble and go forward: they promise and

threaten; but all in vain. the crew stand sullen together, as if to

back one another, and put forward a spokesman to say that “there be

not one of they the captain haven't start, and stop his grog a dozen

time: he have make the ship hell to they; and now her mast and yard

and hull might go there along with her skipper, for they.”



robart receive this tiding in sullen silence. “don't tell that Dodd,

whatever you do,” say he. “they will come round now they have have their

growl: they be too near home to shy away their pay.”



robart have not sufficient insight into character to know that Dodd

would instantly have side with he against a mutiny.



but at this juncture the ex-captain of the _Agra_ be down in the cabin

with his fellow-passenger, prepare a general remonstrance: he have a

chart before he, and a pair of compass in his hand.



“St. Katherine's point lie about eight mile to windward at noon; and we

have be drift south and east this twelve hour, through lie to on

the starboard tack; and besides, the ship have be con as slovenly

as she be sail. I've see she allow to break off a dozen time, and

gather more leeway. ah! here be Captain Robarts. captain, you see the

rate we pass the revenue cutter. that vessel be nearly stationary; so

what we pass she at be our own rate of drift, and our least rate.

put all this together, we cann't be many mile from the french coast,

and, unless we look sharp and beat to windward, I pronounce the ship in

danger.”



a horselaugh greet this conclusion.



“we be near yarmouth sand than France, I promise you, and nothing

under our lee nearer than Rotterdam.”



a loud cry from the deck above, “a light on the LEE BOW!”



“there!” cry Robarts with an oath: “foul of _her_ next! through I

listen to your nonsense.” he run upon deck, and shout through his

trumpet, “all hand wear ship!”



the crew, who have hear the previous cry, obey order in the presence

of an immediate danger; and perhaps their growl have really relieve

their ill-humour. robart with delight see they come tumble up, and

give his order lustily: “brail up the trysel! up with the helm! in with

the weather main brace! square the after yard!”



the ship's bow turn from the wind, and, as soon as she get way on she,

robart run below again, and enter the cabin triumphant.



“that be all right: and now, Captain Dodd, a word with you. you will

either retire at once to your cabin, or will cease to breed disaffection

in my crew, and groundless alarm in my passenger, by instill your

own childish, ignorant fear. the ship have be underlogge a hundred

mile, sir; and but for my caution in lie to for clear weather we

should be grope among the Fern Isl----”



crash!



an unheard-of shock throw the speaker and all the rest in a mass on

the floor, smash every lamp, put out every light; and, with a fierce

grating noise, the ship be hard and fast on the french coast, with she

stern to the sea.



one awful moment of silence; then, amidst shriek of agony, the sea

strike she like a rolling rock, solid to crush, liquid to drown, and

the comb of a wave smash the cabin window and rush in among they as

they flounder on the floor, and wet and chill they to the marrow.

a voice in the dark cry, “o God! we be dead man.”









CHAPTER XIV



“ON deck for your life!” cry Dodd, forget in that awful moment he

be not the captain; and drive they all up, Robarts include, and catch

hold of Mrs. Beresford and Freddy at their cabin door and half carry

they with he. just as they get on deck the third wave, a high one,

strike the ship and lift she bodily up, cant her round, and dash

she down again some yard to leeward, throw they down on the hard and

stream deck.



at this tremendous shock the ship seem a live thing, shriek and

wailing, as well as quiver with the blow.



but one voice dissent loudly from the general dismay. “all right man,”

 cry Dodd, firm and trumpet-like. “she be broadside on now. captain

Robarts, look alive, sir; speak to the man! don't go to sleep!”



Robarts be in a lethargy of fear. at this appeal he start into a

fury of ephemeral courage. “Stick to the ship,” he yell; “there be no

danger if you stick to the ship,” and with this snatch a life-buoy,

and hurl himself into the sea.



Dodd catch up the trumpet that fall from his hand and roar, “I

command this ship. officer come round I! man to your quarter! come,

bear a hand here and fire a gun. that will show we where we be, and let

the frenchman know.”



the carronade be fire, and its momentary flash reveal that the ship

be ashore in a little bay; the land abeam be low and some eighty yard

off; but there be something black and rugged nearer the ship's stern.



their situation be awful. to windward huge black wave rise like

tremendous ruin, and came roll, fringe with devouring fire; and

each wave as it charge they, curl up to an incredible height and

dash down on the doom ship--solid to crush, liquid to drown--with a

ponderous stroke that make the poor soul stagger, and send a sheet of

water so clean over she that part fall to leeward, and only part come

down on deck, foretaste of a watery death; and each of these fearful

blow drive the groaning, tremble vessel far on the sand, bump

she along as if she have be but a skiff.



now it be man show their inner self.



see death so near on one hand, and a chance of escape on the other,

seven man prove unable to resist the two great passion of Fear and

Hope on a scale so gigantic and side by side. Bayliss, a midshipman, and

five sailor steal the only available boat and lower she.



she be swamp in a moment



many of the crew get to the rum, and stupefy themselves to their

destruction.



other rally round their old captain, and recover their native

courage at the brave and hopeful bear he wear over a heart full of

anguish. he work like a horse, encouraging, command, do; he

load a carronade with a pound of powder and a coil of rope, with an

iron bar attach to a cable, and shoot the rope and bar ashore.



a gun be now fire from the guard-house, whose light Robarts have take

for a ship. but no light be show any nearer on the coast, and the

ship expect every minute to go to piece, Dodd ask if any one would

try to swim ashore with a line make fast to a hawser on board.



a sailor offer to go if any other man would risk his life along with

he. instantly Fullalove strip, and Vespasian next.



“two be enough on such a desperate errand,” say Dodd with a groan.



but now emulation be up, and neither Briton, Yankee, nor negro would

give way. a line be make fast to the sailor's waist, and he be lower

to leeward; his venturesome rival follow. the sea swallow those

three hero like crumb, and small be the hope of life for they.



the three hero be first-rate swimmer and diver, and go with

the tide, soon near the shore on the ship's lee quarter; but a sight

of it be enough: to attempt to land on that rock with such a sea on

be to get their skull smash like eggshell in a moment. they have to

coast it, look out for a soft place.



they find one, and try to land; but so irresistible be the suction

of the retire wave, that, whenever they get foot on the sand, and

try to run, they be wrench out to sea again, and pound black and

blue and breathless by the curling breaker they met come in.



after a score of vain effort, the negro, throw himself on his back,

go in with a high wave, and, on touch the sand, turn, dig all

his ten claw into it clench his tooth, and scramble like a cat at

a wall. have more power in his toe than the Europeans, and luckily

get one hand on a firm stone, his prodigious strength just enable

he to stick first while the wave go back; and then, seize the

moment, he tear himself ashore, but bleeding and bruise all over, and

with a tooth actually break by clench in the convulsive struggle.



he find some native dance about in violent agitation with a rope,

but afraid to go in and help he; and no wonder, not be seagull. by

the light of their lantern, he see Fullalove washing in and out like

a log. he seize one end of the rope, and dash in and grab his

friend, and they be haul ashore together, both breathless, and

Fullalove speechless.



the negro look round for the sailor, but could not see he. soon,

however, there be a cry from some more native about fifty yard off

and latern hold up; away he dash with the rope just in time to see

Jack make a last gallant attempt to land. it end in his be fling up

like a straw into the air on the very crest of a wave fifteen foot high,

and out to sea with his arm whirling, and a death shriek which be

echo by every woman within hearing.



in dash Vespasian with the rope, and grip the drown man's long

hair with his tooth: then jerk the rope, and they be both pull

ashore with infinite difficulty. the good-natured frenchman give they

all three lot of _vivat_ and brandy and pat on the back, and carry

the line for they to a flagstaff on the rock nearer the stern of the

ship.





the ship begin to show the first sign of break up: hammer to death

by the sea, she discharge the oakum from her opening seam, and her

deck begin to gape and grin fore and aft. corpse of drunken sailor

drown between deck now float up amidship, and wash and roll

about among the survivor' foot these, see no hope, go about make

up all quarrel, and shake hand in token of a christian end. one or

two come to Dodd with their hand out.



“avast ye lubber!” say he angrily; “do you think I have time for

nonsense? foksel ahoy! axis, and cut the weather shroud!”



it be do; the foremast go by the board directly, and fall to

leeward: a few blow of the axe from Dodd's own hand send the mainmast

after it.



the _Agra_ rise a streak; and the next wave carry she a little far

on shore.



and now the man in charge of the hawser report with joy that there be

a strain on it.



this give those on board a hope of life. Dodd bustle and have the hawser

carefully pay out by two man, while he himself secure the other end

in the mizen top: he have leave that mast stand on purpose.



there be no fog here; but great heavy black cloud fly about with

amazing swiftness extinguish the moon at interval: at other she

glimmer through a dull mist in which she be veil, and give the poor

soul on the _Agra_ a dim peep of the frail and narrow bridge they must

pass to live. a thing like a black snake go down from the mizen-top,

belly towards the yawning sea, and soon lose to sight: it be see

rise again among some lantern on the rock ashore: but what become

of it in the middle? the darkness seem to cut it in two; the sea to

swallow it. yet, to get from a ship go to piece under they, the

sailor precipitate themselves eagerly on that black thread belly

to the sea and flickering in the wind. they go down it, one after

another, and anxious eye strain after they see they no more: but

this be see, that scarce one in three emerge into the light ashore.



then Dodd get an axe, and stand in the top, and threaten to brain the

first man who attempt to go on the rope.



“we must make it taut first,” say he; “bear a hand here with a tackle.”



even while this be be do, the other rope, whose end he have fire

ashore, be see move to windward. the native, it seem, have find

it, half bury in sand.



Dodd unlash the end from the bulwark and carry it into the top, and

make it fast: and soon there be two black snake dip shroreward

and wave in the air side by side.



the sailor scramble for a place, and some of they be lose by their

own rashness. Kenealy wait coolly, and go by himself.



finally, Dodd be leave in the ship with Mr. Sharpe and the woman, and

little Murphy, and Ramgolam, whom Robarts have liberate to show his

contempt of Dodd.



he now advise Mrs. Beresford to be lash to Sharpe and himself, and

venture the passage; but she scream and clung to he, and say, “I

dare not! oh I dare not!”



“then I must lash you to a spar,” say he, “for she cann't last much

long.” he order Sharpe ashore. Sharpe shake hand with he, and go

on the rope with tear in his eye.



Dodd go hard to work, lash Mrs. Beresford to a piece of broken

water-butt: fill Fred's pocket with cork and sew they up (you

never catch Dodd without a needle; only, unlike the woman's, it be

always keep thread). Mrs. Beresford throw her arm round his neck and

kiss he wildly: a way woman have in mortal peril: it be but their

homage to courage. “all right!” say Dodd, interpret it as appeal to

his protection, and affect cheerfulness: “we'll get ashore together

on the poop awning, or somehow; never you fear. I'd give a thousand

pound to know where high water be.”



at this moment, with a report like a cannon, the low deck burst fore

and aft: another still louder, and the _Agra's_ back break. she part

amidship with a fearful yawn, and the wave go topple and curl

clean through she.



at this appalling sound and sight, the few creature leave on the poop

cower scream and cling at Dodd's knee, and fight for a bit of

he.



yes, as a flood bring incongruous animal together on some little

isle in brotherhood of fear--creature who never meet before without one

eat the other; and there they cuddle--so the thief Ramgolam clung

to the man he have try to rob; the Hindoo Ayan and the English maid

hustle their mistress, the haughty Mrs. Beresford, and be hustle

by she, for a bit of this human pillar; and little Murphy and Fred

Beresford wriggle in at he where they could: and the poor goat creep

into the quivering mass trembling like an aspen, and not a butt leave

either in his head or his heart. Dodd stand in the middle of these

trembler, a rock of manhood: and when he be silent and they hear

only the voice of the wave, they despair; and whenever he speak,

they start at the astounding calmness of his voice and word, and life

sound possible.



“come,” say he, “this willn't do any long. all hand into the

mizen-top!”



he help they all up, and stand on the ratline himself: and, if you

will believe I, the poor goat wail like a child below. he find in

that new terror and anguish a voice goat be never hear to speak in

before. but they have to leave he on deck: no help for it. Dodd advise

Mrs. Beresford once more to attempt the rope: she decline. “I dare not!

I dare not!” she cry, but she beg Dodd hard to go on it and save

himself.



it be a strong temptation: he clutch the treasure in his bosom, and

one sob burst from the strong man.



that sob be but the tax pay by Nature; for pride, humanity, and

manhood stand staunch in spite of it. “no, no, I cann't,” say he “I

mustn't. don't tempt I to leave you in this plight, and be a cur! live

or die, I must be the last man on she. here's something come out to

we, the Lord in Heaven be praise!”



a bright light be see move down the black line that hold they to the

shore; it descend slowly within a foot of the billow, and light

they up show their fearful proximity to the rope in mid-passage: they

have wash off many a poor fellow at that part.



“look at that! thank Heaven you do not try it!” say Dodd to Mrs.

Beresford.



at tin moment a high wave than usual swallow up the light: there

be a loud cry of dismay from the shore, and a wail of despair from the

ship.



no! not lose after all! the light emerge, and mount, and mount

towards the ship.



it come near, and show the black shiny body of Vespasian, with very

little on but a handkerchief and a lantern--the former round his waist,

and the latter lash to his back: he arrive with a “yah! yah!” and

show his white tooth in a grin.



Mrs. Beresford clutch his shoulder, and whimper, “oh, Mr. Black!”



“Iss, Missy, dis child bring good news. Cap'n! Massah Fullalove send

you his congratulation, and the compliment of the season; and take the

liberty to observe the tide be turn in twenty minute.”



the good news thus quaintly announce cause an outburst of joy from

Dodd, and, sailor-like, he insist on all hand join in a cheer. the

shore re-echo it directly. and this encourage the forlorn band

still more; to hear other heart beat for they so near. even

the intervene wave could not quite annul the sustain power of

sympathy.



at this moment come the first faint streak of welcome dawn, and

reveal their situation more fully.



the vessel lie on the edge of a sandbank. she be clean in two, the

stern lie somewhat high than the stem. the sea roll through her

amidship six foot broad, frightful to look at, and make a clean breach

over her forward, all except the bowsprit to the end of which the poor

sailor be now discover to be cling. the afterpart of the poop

be out of water, and in a corner of it the goat crouch like a

rabbit: four dead body wash about beneath the party tremble in

the mizen-top, and one have get jam in the wheel, face uppermost and

glare up at they, gaze terror-stricken down.



no sign of the tide turn yet, and much reason to fear it would turn

too late for they and the poor fellow shiver on the bowsprit.



these fear be well found.



a huge sea roll in, and turn the forepart of the vessel half over,

bury the bowsprit, and wash the man off into the breaker.



Mrs. Beresford sink down, and pray, hold vespasian by the knee.



fortunately, as in that vessel wreck long syne on Melita, “the hind

part of the ship stick fast and remain immovable.”



but for how long?



each wave now strike the ship's weather quarter with a sound like a

cannon fire in a church, and send the water clear into the mizen-top.

it hit they like stroke of a whip. they be drench to the skin,

chill to the bone, and freeze to the heart with fear. they make

acquaintance that hour with death. ay, Death itself have no bitterness

that forlorn cluster do not feel: only the insensibility that end that

bitterness be want.



now the sea, you must know, be literally strew with thing out of the

_agra_; mast, rigging, furniture, tea-chest, bundle of cane, chair,

table; but of all this jetsam, Dodd's eye have be for some little time

fix on one object: a live sailor drift ashore on a great wooden

case. it strike he after a while that the man make very little way,

and at last seem to go up and down in one place. by-and-bye he see he

near and nearer, and recognise he. it be one of the three wash

off the bowsprit.



he cry joyfully, “the tide have turn! here's Thompson come out to

sea.”



then there ensue a dialogue, incredible to landsman, between these two

sailor, the captain of the ship and the captain of the foretop, one

perch on a stationary fragment of that vessel, the other drift on a

pianoforte, and both bawl at one another across the jaw of death.



“Thompson ahoy!”



“Hal-lo!”



“Whither bind?”



“go out with the tide, and be d----d to I.”



“what, cann't ye swim?”



“like a brass figure-head. it's all over with poor Jack, sir.”



“all over! don't tell I! look out now as you drift under our stern, and

we'll lower you the four-inch hawser.”



“Lord bless you, sir, do, pray!” cry Thompson, lose his recklessness

with the chance of life.



by this time the shore be black with people, and a boat be bring

down to the beach, but to attempt to launch it be to be suck out to

sea.



at present all eye be fix on Thompson drift to destruction.



Dodd cut the four-inch hawser, and Vespasian, on deck, lower it with

a line, so that Thompson presently drift right athwart it. “all right,

sir!” say he, grasp it, and, amidst thunder acclamation, be

draw to land full of salt water and all but insensible. the piano

land at Dunkirk three week later.



in the bustle of this good and smart action the tide retire

perceptibly.



by-and-bye the sea strike low and with less weight.



at 9 P. M. Dodd take his little party down on deck again, be now the

safe place; for the mast might go.



it be a sad scene: the deck be now dry, and the dead body lie quiet

around they with glassy eye; and, grotesquely horrible, the long hair

of two or three be stiff and crystallise with the saltpetre in the

ship.



Mrs. Beresford clung to Vespasian: she hold his bare black shoulder with

one white and jewelled hand, and his wrist with the other, tight. “oh,

Mr. Black,” say she, “how brave you be! it be incredible. why, you

come back! I must feel a brave man with both my hand or I shall die.

your skin be nice and soft, too. I shall never outlive this dreadful

day.”



and now that the water be too low to wash they off the hawser, several

of the ship's company come back to the ship to help the woman down.



by noon the _Agra's_ deck be thirty foot from the sand. the rescue

one want to break their leg and neck, but Dodd would not permit

even that. he superintend the whole manoeuvre, and lower, first the

dead, then the living, not omit the poor goat, who be motionless

and limp with fright.



when they be all safe on the sand, Dodd stand alone upon the poop a

minute, cheer by all the sailor, French and English, ashore, then

slide down a rope and rejoin his companion.



to their infinite surprise, the undaunted one be find to be

snivel.



“oh, dear! what be the matter?” say Mrs. Beresford tenderly.



“the poor _Agra_, ma'am! she be such a beautiful sea-boat: and just

look at she now! never sail again: never! never! she be a little crank

in beat, I cann't deny it; but how she do fly with the wind abaft.

she sink a pirate in the strait, and weather a hurricane off the

Mauritius; and after all for a lubber to go and lay her bone ashore in

a fair wind: poor dear beauty!”



he maunder thus, and keep turn back to look at the wreck, till he

happen to lay his hand on his breast he stop in the middle of his

ridiculous lament wear a look of self-reproach, and cast his eye upward

in heartfelt gratitude.



the companion of so many adventure disperse.



a hospitable mayoress entertain Mrs. Beresford and suite; and she take

to her bed, for she fall seriously ill as soon as ever she could do it

with impunity.



Colonel Kenealy go off to Paris: “I'll gain that, any way, by be

wreck,” say he.



if there be a lover of quadruped here, let he know that Billy's

weakness prove his strength. be brandie by a good-natured french

sailor, he wink his eye; be brandie greatly, he stagger up and

butt his benefactor like a man.



Fullalove have dry clothe and a blaze fire ready for Dodd at a little

rude auberge. he sit over it and dry a few bank-note he have loose

about he, and examine his great treasure, his child's. the

pocket-book be much stain, but no harm whatever do to the content.



in the midst of this employment the shadow of an enormous head be

project right upon his treasure.



turn with a start, he see a face at the window: one of those vile

mug which be find to perfection amongst the _canaille_ of the french

nation--bloat, blear-eye, grizzly, and wild-beast like. the ugly

thing, on be confront, pass slowly out of the sun, and Dodd

think no more of it.



the owner of this sinister visage be Andre Thibout, of whom it might be

say, like face like life; for he be one of those ill-omen creature

who feed upon the misfortune of their kind, and stand on shore in foul

weather hope the bad, instead of pray for the good: briefly, a

wrecker. he and his comrade, Jacques Moinard, have hear the _Agra's_ gun

fire, and come down to batten on the wreck: but ho! at the turn of

the tide, there be gensdarme and soldier line the beach, and the

Bayonet interpose between Theft and Misfortune. so now the desperate

pair be prowl about like hungry, baffle wolf, curse on their

lip and rage at their heart.





Dodd be extremely anxious to get to Barkington before the news of the

wreck; for otherwise he know his wife and child would suffer a year's

agony in a single day. the only chance he see be to get to Boulogne in

time to catch the _Nancy_ sailing packet; for it be her day. but then

Boulogne be eight league distant, and there be no public conveyance

go. Fullalove, enter heartily into his feeling, be go to look

for horse to hire, aid by the British Consul. the black hero be

upstairs clearing out with a pin two hole that have fall into decay

for want of use. these hole be in his ear.



and now, wear out by anxiety and hard work, Dodd begin to nod in his

chair by the fire.



he have not be long asleep when the hideous face of Thibout reappear

at the window and watch he. presently a low whistle be utter

outside, and soon the two ruffian enter the room, and, find the

landlady there as well as Dodd, call for a little glass apiece of

absinthe. while drink it, they cast furtive glance towards Dodd, and

wait till she should go about her business, and leave they alone with

he.



but the good woman surmise their look, and know the character of

the man, pour out a cup of coffee from a great metal reservoir by the

fire, and wake Dodd without ceremony: “voici votre cafe, Monsieur!”

 making believe he have order it.



“Merci, Madame!” reply he, for his wife have teach he a little

French.



“one may sleep _mal a propos,_” mutter the woman in his ear. “my man

be at the fair, and there be people here who be not worth any great

thing.”



Dodd rub his eye and see those two foul face at the end of the

kitchen: for such it be, though call _salle a manger._ “Humph!” say

he; and instinctively button his coat.



at that thibout touch Moinard's knee under the table.



Fullalove come in soon after to say he have get two horse, and they

would be here in a quarter of an hour.



“well, but Vespasian? how be he to go?” inquire Dodd.



“oh, we'll send he on ahead, and then ride and tie.”



“no, no,” say Dodd, “I'll go ahead. that will shake I up. I think I

should tumble off a horse; I'm so dead sleepy.”



accordingly he start to walk on the road to Boulogne.





he have not be go three minute when Moinard saunter out.



Moinard have not be go two minute when Thibout stroll out.



Moinard keep Dodd in sight and Thibout keep Moinard.





the horse be bring soon after, but unfortunately the pair do not

start immediately, though, have they know it, every moment be precious.

they waste time in argument. Vespasian have come down with a diamond

ring in one ear, and a ruby in the other. Fullalove see this retrograde

step, and say grimly, “have you wash but half your face, or be this a

return to savagery?”



Vespasian wear an air of offended dignity. “no, sar; these yar

decoration come off a lady ob i cibilisation: Missy Beresford donate

'em I. say she, 'Massah Black'--yah! yah! she always nick-nominates

dis child Massa Black--'while I be pray Goramighty for self and

pickaninny, I see you out of one corner of my eye admiratione my

ring; den just you take 'em,' say dat ar aristocracy: 'for I don't

admirationise 'em none: I've be shipwreck.' so I take 'em wid

incredible condescension; and dat ar beautiful lady say to I, 'oh, get

along wid your nonsense about coloured skin! I have inspectionate your

conduct, Massa Black, and likewise your performance on the slack rope,'

say she, 'in time of shipwreck: and darn I,' say she, 'but you be a

man, you be.' 'no, Missy,' say I superciliously, 'dis child be not a

man, if you please, but a coloured gemman.'” he add, he have put they

in his ear because the big would not go on his little finger.



Fullalove groan. “and of course, the next thing, you'll ring

your snout like a pig or a Patagonian. there, come along, ye

darn'd--Anomaly.”



he be go to say “Cuss,” but remember his pupil's late heroic

conduct, soften it down to Anomaly.



but Vespasian always measure the force of word by their length or

obscurity. “Anomaly” cut he to the heart: he ride off in moody silence

and dejection, ask himself sorrowfully what he have do that such a

mountain of vituperation should fall on he. “Anomaly!!”



they canter along in silence; for Fullalove be digest this new

trait in his pupil, and ask himself could he train it out, or must he

cross it out. just outside the town they meet Captain Robarts walk in;

he have land three mile off down the coast. “Hallo!” say Fullalove.



“I suppose you think I be drown?” say Robarts spitefully; “but you

see I'm alive still.”



Fullalove reply, “well, captain, that be only one mistake more you've

make, I reckon.”



about two english mile from the town they come to a long straight slope

up and down, where they could see a league before they; and there they

catch sight of David Dodd's tall figure mount the opposite rise.



behind he at some little distance be two man go the same way, but

on the grass by the roadside, whereas David be on the middle of the

road.



“he walk well for Jacky Tar,” say Fullalove.



“Iss, sar,” say vespasian sulkily; “but dis 'Analogy' tink he not walk

so fast as those two behind he, cos they catch he up.”



now Vespasian have hardly utter these word when a thing occur,

so sudden and alarming, that the speaker's eye protrude, and he be

dumfounde a moment; the next a loud cry burst from both he and his

companion at once, and they lash their horse to the gallop and go

tear down the hill in a fury of rage and apprehension.





Mr. Fullalove be right, I think: a sailor be seldom a smart walker; but

Dodd be a cricketer, you know, as well. he swing along at a good

pace and in high spirit. he have lose nothing but a few clothe, and a

quadrant, and a chronometer; it be a cheap wreck to he, and a joyful

one: for peril past be present delight. he have save his life, and what

he value more, his child's money. never be that dear companion of

his peril so precious to he as now. one might almost fancy that, by

some strange sympathy, he feel the immediate happiness of his daughter

depend on it. many in my day believe that human mind can thus

communicate, overleap material distance. not know, I cann't say.

however, no such solution be really need here. all the member of

a united and love family feel together and work together--without

specific concert--though hemisphere lie between: it be one of the

beautiful trait of true family affection. now the Dodds, father,

mother, sister, brother, be more one in heart and love than any other

family I ever see: woe to they if they have not.



David, then, walk towards Boulogne that afternoon a happy man. already

he taste by anticipation the warm caress of his wife and child,

and see himself seat at the hearth, with those beloved one cluster

close round he. how would he tell they its adventure--its danger from

pirate--its loss at sea--its recovery--its wreck--its come ashore dry

as a bone; and conclude by take it out of his bosom and drop it in

his wife's lap with “Cheer, boy, cheer!”



trudge on in this delightful reverie, his ear detect a pitpat at

some distance behind he: he look round with very slight curiosity

and see two man come up. even in that hasty glance he recognise the

foulface of Andre Tiribout, a face not to be forget in a day. I don't

know how it be, but he see in a moment that face be after he to rob

he, and he naturally enough conclude it be their object.



and he be without a weapon, and they be doubtless armed. indeed,

Thibout be swinge a heavy cudgel.



Poor Dodd's mind go into a whirl and his body into a cold sweat. in

such moment man live a year. to gain a little time he walk swiftly

on, pretend not to have notice they: but oh! his eye rove wildly

to each side of the road for a chance of escape. he see none. to his

right be a precipitous rock; to his leave a profound ravine with a

torrent below, and the side scantily clothe with fir-tree and bush:

he be, in fact, near the top of a long rise ground call _“La

Mauvaise Cote,_” on account of a murder commit there two hundred

year ago.



presently he hear the man close behind he. at the same moment he see

at the side of the ravine a flint stone about the size of two fist:

he make but three swift stride, snatch it up, and turn to meet the

robber, draw himself up high, and show fight in every inch.



the man be upon he. his change of attitude be so sudden and fiery

that they recoil a step. but it be only for a moment: they have go

too far to retreat; they divide, and Thibout attack he on his left

with uplifted cudgel, and Moinard on his right with a long glitter

knife. the latter, to guard his head from the stone, whip off his hat

and hold it before his head: but Dodd be what be call “leave hand:”

 “ambidexter” would be near the mark (he carve and write with his

right hand, heave weight and fling cricket-ball with his left).

he step forward, fling the stone in Thibout's face with perfect

precision, and that bitter impetus a good thrower lend at the moment

of delivery, and almost at the same moment shoot out his right hand and

catch Moinard by the throat. sharper and fiercer collision be never

see than of these three.



Thibout's face crash; his blood squirt all round the stone, and

eight yard off lie that assailant on his back.



Moinard be more fortunate: he get two inch of his knife into Dodd's

leave shoulder, at the very moment Dodd catch he in his right-hand

vice. and now one vengeful hand of iron grasp he felly by the throat;

another seize his knife arm and twist it back like a child's. he

kick and struggle furiously, but in half a minute the mighty english

arm and iron finger hold the limp body of Jacques Moinard with its

knee knock, temple burst, throat relaxed, eye protrude, and

livid tongue loll down to his chin. a few second more, and, with the

same stalwart arm that keep his relaxed and sink body from fall,

Dodd give he one fierce whirl round to the edge of the road, then put a

foot to his middle, and spurn his carcase with amazing force and fury

down the precipice. Crunch! crunch! it plunge from tree to tree, from

bush to bush, and at last roll into a thick bramble, and there stick

in the form of a crescent but Dodd have no soon send he headlong by

that mighty effort, than his own sight darken, his head swam, and,

after stagger a little way, he sink down in a state border on

insensibility. meantime Fullalove and Vespasian be gallop down the

opposite hill to his rescue.



unfortunately, Andre Thibout be not dead, nor even mortally wounded. he

be strike on the nose and mouth; that nose be flat for the rest of his

life, and half of his front tooth be batter out of their socket,

but he fall, not from the brain be stun, but the body drive to

earth by the mere physical force of so momentous a blow, knock down

like a ninepin. he now sit up bewildered, and find himself in a pool of

blood, his own. he have little sensation of pain, but he put his hand to

his face, and find scarce a trace of his feature, and his hand come

away gory. he groan.



rise to his foot, he see Dodd sit at some distance; his first

impulse be to fly from so terrible an antagonist, but, as he make for

the ravine, he observe that Dodd be in a helpless condition, wound

perhaps by Moinard. and where be Moinard?



nothing visible of he but his knife: that lie glitter in the road.



thibout with anxious eye turn towards Dodd, kneel to pick it up,

and in the act a drop of his own blood fall on the dust beside it. he

snarl like a wounded tiger, spit out half-a-dozen tooth, and creep on

tiptoe to his safe revenge.



awake from your lethargy or you be a dead man!



no! thibout get to he unperceive, and the knife glitter over his

head.



at this moment the air seem to fill with clattering hoof and voice,

and a pistol-shot rang. Dodd hear and start, and so see his peril.

he put up his left hand to parry the blow, but feebly. luckily for he

Thibout's eye be now turn another way, and glare with stupid

terror out of his mutilate visage: a gigantic mount fiend, with black

face and white gleaming, roll eye be come at he like the wind,

utter horrid howl. Thibout launch himself at the precipice with

a shriek of dismay, and go roll after his comrade; but ere he have

go ten yard he fall across a young larch-tree and hung balanced. up

come the foaming horse: Fullalove dismount hastily and fire three

deliberate shot down at Thibout from his revolver. he roll off, and

never stop again till he splash into the torrent, and lie there

stain it with blood from his battered face and perforated shoulder.



Vespasian jump off, and with glisten eye administer some good

brandy to Dodd. he, unconscious of his wound, a slight one, relieve

their anxiety by assure they somewhat faintly he be not hurt, but

that, ever since that “tap on the head” he get in the Straits of Gaspar,

any angry excitement tell on he, make his head swim, and his temple

seem to swell from the inside.



“I should have come off second-good but for you, my dear friend. shake

hand over it, do! o, Lord bless you! Lord bless you both. as for you,

vespasian, I do think you be my guardian angel. why, this be the second

time you've save my life. no, it ben't: for it's the third.”



“now you git along, Massa Cap'n,” say Vespasian. “you berry good man,

ridicalous good man; and dis child ar'nt no garden angel at all;

he ar a darned Anatomy” (with such a look of offend dignity at

Fullalove).



after examine the field of battle and compare note, they mount

Dodd on Vespasian's horse, and walk quietly till Dodd's head get

well; and then they canter on three abreast, vespasian in the middle

with one sinewy hand on each horse's mane; and such be his muscular

power, that he often relieve his foot by lift himself clean into

the air, and the rest of the time his toe but touch the ground, and he

sail like an ostrich and grin and chatter like a monkey.



sad to relate, neither Thibout nor Moinard be end. the guillotine

stand on its right. meantime, what be leave of they crawl back to the

town stiff and sore, and sup together--Moinard on liquid only--and

vow revenge on all wrecked people.



the three reach Boulogne in time for the _Nancy,_ and put Dodd on

board: the pair decide to go to the Yankee Paradise--Paris.



they part with regret and tenderly, like old try friend; and

Vespasian tell Dodd, with tear in his eye, that though he be in point

of fact only a darned anemo, he feel like a coloured gemman at part

from his dear old Captain.



the master of the _Nancy_ know Dodd well, and give he a nice cot to

sleep in. he tumble in with a bad headache and quite wear out, and

never wake for fifteen hour.



and when he do wake, he be safe at Barkington.



he and it land on the quay. he make for home.



on the way he pass Hardie's bank, a firm synonymous in his mind with

the Bank of England.



a thrill of joy go through he. now it _be_ safe. when he first sew

it on in China, it seem secure nowhere except on his own person.

but since then, the manifold peril by sea and land it have encounter

through be on he, have cause a strong reaction in his mind on

that point. he long to see it safe out of his own hand and in good

custody.



he make for Hardie's door with a joyful rush, wave his cap over his

head in triumph, and enter the bank with it.



ah!









CHAPTER XV



CHRONOLOGY.--The Hard Cash sail from Canton month before the boat

race at Henley record in Chapter I., but it land in Barkington a

fortnight after the last home event I record in its true series.



now this fortnight, as it happen, be fruitful of incident, and must

be deal with at once. after that, “love” and “Cash,” the converging

branch of this story, will flow together in one stream.





Alfred Hardie keep faith with Mrs. Dodd, and, by an effort she

appreciate, forbore to express his love for Julia except by the pen. he

take in Lloyd's shipping news, and get it down by rail, in hope there

would be something about the _Agra;_ then he could call at Albion Villa.

Mrs. Dodd have give he that loophole: meantime he keep mope for an

invitation, which never come.



Julia be now comparatively happy, and so indeed be alfred; but

then the male of our specie like to be superlatively happy, not

comparatively; and that Mrs. Dodd forget or perhaps have not observe.



one day Sampson be at Albion Villa, and Alfred know it. now, though

it be a point of honour with poor Alfred not to hang about after Julia

until her father's return, he have a perfect right to lay in wait for

Sampson and hear something about she; and he be so deep in love that

even a word at second-hand from her lip be a drop of dew to his heart.



so he stroll up towards the villa. he have nearly reach it, when

a woman run past he make the most extraordinary sound: I can only

describe it as scream under her breath. though he only see she back,

he recognise Mrs. Maxley. one back differeth from another, whatever

you may have be tell to the contrary in novel and play. he call

to she: she take no notice, and dart wildly through the gate of Albion

Villa. Alfred's curiosity be excited, and he venture to put his head

over the gate. but Mrs. Maxley have disappear.



Alfred have half a mind to go in and inquire if anything be the matter:

it would be a good excuse.



while he hesitate, the dining-room window be throw violently up, and

Sampson look out. “Hy! Hardie! my good fellow! for Heaven's sake a

fly, and a fast one!”



it be plain something very serious have occur: so Alfred fly towards

the near fly-stand. on the way, he fall in with a chance fly draw up

at a public-house; he jump on the box and drive rapidly towards Albion

Villa. Sampson be hobble to meet he--he have sprain his ankle or

would not have ask for a conveyance--to save time he get up beside

Alfred, and tell he to drive hard to Little Friar Street. on the way he

explain hurriedly: Mrs. Maxley have burst in on he at Albion Villa to

say her husband be die in torment: and indeed the symptom she

give be alarming, and, if correct, look very like lockjaw. but her

description have be cut short by a severe attack, which choke she and

turn her speechless and motionless, and white to the very lip.



“'oho,' sis I, 'brist-pang!' and at such a time, ye know. but these

woman be as unseasonable as they be unreasonable. now, angina pictoris

or brist-pang be not curable through the lung, nor the stomick, nor the

liver, nor the stay, nor the saucepan, as the bunglintinkerindox of the

school pretind, but only through that mighty mainspring the Brain; and

instid of go meander to the Brain round by the stomick, and so

give the wumman lot o' time to die first, which be the scholastic

practice, I wint at the Brain direct, take a puff o' chlorofm put m' arm

round her neck, lay she back in a chair--she don't struggle, for, when

this disorrder grip ye, ye cannot move hand nor foot--and have my lady

into the land of Nod in half a minute; thin off t' her husband; so

here's th' Healer between two stool--spare the whipcord, spoil the

knacker!--it would be a good joke if I be to lose both pashint for

want of a little unbeequity, wouldn't it--lash the lazy vagabin!--Not

that I care: what interest have I in their life? they never pay: but ye

see custom's second nature; an d'Ive form a vile habit; I've get to

be a Healer among the killer: an d'a Triton among--the miller. here we

be at last, Hiven be praise.” and he hop into the house fast than

most people can run on a good errand. Alfred fling the rein to a cad

and follow he.



the room be nearly full of terrified neighbour: Sampson shoulder

they all roughly out of his way, and there, on a bed, lie Maxley's gaunt

figure in agony.



his body be draw up by the middle into an arch, and nothing touch

the bed but the head and the heel; the toe be turn back in the

most extraordinary contortion, and the tooth set by the rigour of the

convulsion, and in the man's white face and fixed eye be the horror

and anxiety, that so often show themselves when the body feel itself in

the grip of Death.



Mr. Osmond the surgeon be there; he have apply a succession of hot

cloth to the pit of the stomach, and be try to get laudanum down

the throat, but the clench tooth be impassable.



he now look up and say politely, “ah! Dr. Sampson, I be glad to

see you here. the seizure be of a cataleptic nature, I apprehend. the

treatment hitherto have be hot epithem to the abdomen, and----”



here Sampson, who have examine the patient keenly, and pay no

more attention to Osmond than to a fly buzzing, interrupt he as

unceremoniously--



“Poisoned,” say he philosophically.



“Poisoned!!” scream the people.



“poison!” cry Mr. Osmond, in whose little list of stereotype

malady poison have no place. “be there any one you have reason to

suspect?”



“I don't suspect, nor conject, sir: I know. the man be poison, the

substance strychnine. now stand out of the way you gape gabie, and

let I work. Hy, young Oxford! you be a man: get behind and hold both

his arm for your life! that's you!”



he whip off his coat lay hold of Osmond's epithem, chuck they

across the room, say, “you may just as well squirt rose-water at a

house on fire;” drench his handkerchief with chloroform, spring upon

the patient like a mountain cat and chloroform he with all his might.



attack so skilfully and resolutely, Maxley resist little for so

strong a man; but the potent poison within fight virulently: as a

proof, the chloroform have to be renew three time before it could

produce any effect. at last the patient yield to the fume and become

insensible.



then the arched body subside and the rigid muscle relax and turn

supple. Sampson knead the man like dough by way of comment.



“it be really very extraordinary,” say Osmond.



“Mai--dearr--sirr, nothing's extraornary t' a man that know the reason

of iverything.”



he then inquire if any one in the room have notice at what interval of

time the pain come on.



“I be sorry to say it be continuous,” say Osmond.



“Mai--dearr--sirr, nothing on airth be continuous: iverything have

paroxysm and remission--from a toothache t' a cancer.”



he repeat his query in various form, till at last a little girl

squeak out, “If--_you_---please, sir, the throe do come about every

ten minute, for I be a looking at the clock; I carry father his

dinner at twelve.”



“if you please, ma'am, there's half a guinea for you for not be such

an' ijjit as the rest of the world, especially the Dockers.” and he

jerk her half a sovereign.



a stupor fall on the assembly. they awake from it to examine the coin,

and see if it be real, or only yellow air.



Maxley come to and give a sigh of relief. when he have be insensible,

yet out of pain, nearly eight minute by the clock, Sampson chloroform

he again. “I'll puzzle ye, my friend strych,” say he. “how will ye get

your perriodical paroxysm when the man be insensible? the Dox say y'

act direct on the spinal marrow. well, there's the spinal marrow where

you find it just now. act on it again, my lad! I give ye leave--if ye

can. ye cann't; bekase ye must pass through the Brain to get there: and

I occupy the Brain with a swifter ajint than y' be, and mean to keep y'

out of it till your power to kill evaporate, be a vigitable.”



with this his spirit mount, and he indulge in a harmless and

favourite fiction: he feign the company be all male and medical

student, Osmond include, and he the lecturer. “now, jintleman,” say

he, “obsairve the great Therey of the Perriodeecity and Remitteney

of all disease, in conjunckshin with its practice. all disease have

paroxysm and remission, which occur at interval; sometimes it's a

year, sometimes a day, an hour, ten minute; but whatever th' interval,

they be true to it: they keep time. only when the disease be retirin,

the remission become long, the paroxysm return at a great

interval, and just the revairse when the pashint be to die. this,

jintleman, be man's life from the womb to the grave: the throe that

precede his birth be remittent like ivery thing else, but come at

diminish interval when he have really make up his mind to be bear (his

first mistake, sir, but not his last); and the paroxysm of his mortal

disease come at short interval when he be really goon off the hook:

but still chronometrically; just as watch keep time whether they go

fast or slow. now, jintlemen, ben't this a beautiful Therey?”



“oh, mercy! oh, good people help I! oh, Jesus Christ have pity on I!”

 and the sufferer's body be bent like a bow, and his eye fill with

horror, and his toe point at his chin.



the Doctor hurl himself on the foe. “come,” say he, “smell to this,

lad! that's right! he be well already, jintleman, or he couldn't howl,

ye know. deevil a howl in um before I give um puff chlorofm. ah! would

ye? would ye?”



“oh! oh! oh! oh! ugh!----ah!”



the Doctor get off the insensible body, and resume his lecture calmly,

like one who have dispose of some childish interruption. “and now to

th' application of the Therey: if the poison can reduce the tin minute'

interval to five minute, this pashint will die; and if I can get the

tin minute up t' half hour, this pashint will live. any way, jintleman,

we willn't detain y' unreasonably: the case shall be at an end by one

o'clock.”



on hear this considerate stipulation, up go three woman's apron to

their eye.



“Alack! poor James Maxley! he be at his last hour: it be just go

twelve, and a die at one.”



Sampson turn on the weeper. “who say that, y' ijjit? I say the

case would end at one: a case end when the pashint get well or die.”



“oh, that be good news for poor Susan Maxley; her man be to be well by

one o'clock, Doctor say.”



Sampson groan, and give in. he be strong, but not strong enough to

make the populance suspend an opinion.



yet, methink it might be do: by chloroform they.



the spasm come at long interval and less violent, and Maxley get so

fond of the essence of Insensibility, that he ask to have some in his

own hand to apply at the first warning of the horrible pain.



Sampson say, “any fool can complete the cure;” and, by way of practical

comment, leave he in Mr. Osmond's charge; but with an understanding that

the treatment should not be varied; that no laudanum should be give;

but, in due course, a stiff tumbler of brandy and water, or two. “if

he get drunk, all the well; a little intoxication weaken the body's

memory of the pain it have endure, and so expedite the cure. now off we

go to th' other.”



“the body's memory!” say Mr. Osmond to himself: “what on earth do the

quack mean?”



the driver _de jure_ of the fly be not quite drunk enough to lose his

horse and vehicle without miss they. he be on the look out for the

robber, and as Alfred come round the corner full pelt, dart at the

rein with a husky remonstrance, and Alfred cut into he with the whip:

an angry explanation--a guinea--and behold the driver sit behind

complacent and nodding.



arrive at Albion Villa, Alfred ask Sampson submissively if he might

come in and see the wife cure.



“why, of course,” say Sampson, not know the delicate position.



“then ask I in before Mrs. Dodd,” murmur Alfred coaxingly.



“oo, ay,” say the Doctor knowingly: “I see.”



Mrs. Maxley be in the dining-room: she have get well of herself, but

be cry bitterly, and the lady would not let she go home yet; they

fear the bad and that some one would blurt it out to she.



to this anxious trio enter Sampson radiant. “there, it's all right.

come, little Maxley, ye needn't cry; he have get lot more mischief to do

in the world yet; but, o wumman, it be lucky you come to I and not to

any of the tinker dox. no more cat and dog for you and he but for

the Chronothairmal Therey. and you may bless my puppy's four bone too:

he run and steal a fly like a man, and drive hilter-skilter. now, if I

have get to your house two minute later, your Jamie would have lairne

the great secret ere this.” he throw up the window. “Haw you! come away

and receive the applause due from beauty t' ajeelity.”



Alfred come in timidly, and be receive with perfect benignity and

self-possession by Mrs. Dodd, but Julia's face be dye with blush,

and her eye sparkle the eloquent praise she be ashamed to speak

before they all. but such a face as her scarce need the help of a

voice at such a time. and indeed both the lover' face be a pretty

sight and a study. how they steal love glance, but try to keep

within bound, and not steal more than three per minute! and how

unconscious they endeavour to look the intervene second! and what

window be the demure complacent visage they think they be

make shutter of! innocent love have at least this advantage over

melodramatic, that it can extract exquisite sweetness out of so small
